title: Modi-lookalike’s video falsely shared as PM called out in Rourkela over LPG crisis
body_text:
A video, roughly 9 seconds in length, is viral on social media. It purports to show Prime Minister Narendra Modi in a convoy, in what appears to be a roadshow. A voice can be heard in the background complaining about the unavailability of cooking cylinders: “Gas nahi hai sir, kya karenge humlog?” (There is no gas, sir, what shall we do?)
X user @KhelaHobePart2 posted the clip claiming that the video, from Rourkela, showed a by-stander in a roadshow calling out the Prime Minister for the LPG shortage, leaving onlookers amused. (Archive)
Anti-national shouting
Gas nahi mil raha hai, gas nahi mil raha hai 😂 pic.twitter.com/CUYBErGOOK
— SDutta (@KhelaHobePart2) April 1, 2026
Several users, like Verified handle India With Congress, @ShadowSakshi, @YadavManish1001, @Girijeshv, @TelanganaGalam_ and others also circulated the clip on X, claiming that it showed PM Modi being heckled during a roadshow. (Archives: 1, 2, 3, 4)
Screenshots below:
The clip was also viral on Instagram with the same claim, seeing massive circulation.
Kal Tak (@kaltakdigital.in) a news outlet, posted the clip on April 1. It had been viewed more than 4 million times at the time of this article being written.
Several other Instagram users, like @millattimes, @indianfeed24hrs, @okindians, @m_khan.122, @p5news_up also posted the clip alleging that Prime Minister Narendra Modi had recently been in Rourkela, where he was asked about the LPG crisis by a by-stander.
Fact Check
At first glance, the security personnel in the video appear markedly different from the security detail typically seen accompanying Prime Minister Narendra Modi during public appearances.
Hence to verify the authenticity of the viral video, we broke it down into keyframes.
A reverse image search led us to this Instagram reel, which indicated that the incident, from Rourkela, Odisha, featured a lookalike of PM Modi.
View this post on Instagram
Several other Instagram users, like (@trendylast24hr), (@desh), (@_indiansinfo_), and others, also wrote that the clip featured a lookalike, and not the Prime Minister himself.
We tried to geolocate the place shown in the viral clip and found that the reel had indeed been shot in Rourkela.
For comparisons, see below:
Taking cue from this, we ran relevant keyword searches, and came across this YouTube video, uploaded by Odisha-based TV news channel Kalinga TV, on September 27, 2025. It features a person named Sadananda Naik, who is interviewed for being a splitting image of Narendra Modi.
We found another Instagram reel, featuring Mr. Naik, where he says that he had started attending to his beard around 2017, after being told by his acquaintances that he resembled Narendra Modi.
View this post on Instagram
We also found out that the Prime Minister had last visited Odisha on September 27, 2025, to mark the inauguration of several projects in the town of Jharsuguda. This is the same location where Mr. Naik was interviewed by reporters of Kalinga TV.
Since then, PM Modi has not been to Odisha.
To sum up, the person taking part in a roadshow in Rourkela was not the Prime Minister, but his lookalike Sadananda Naik, who had previously gained media attention for resembling the Prime Minister.
claim_text: Prime Minister Modi spotted at a roadshow, man in crowd complains about unavailability of gas cylinder
original_post_text: Anti-national shouting

title: Pakistan-flagged oil tanker destroyed in Hormuz? Unrelated video falsely shared
body_text:
Amid the escalating conflict in West Asia, a video showing a vessel engulfed in flames and billowing smoke is being widely shared across social media platforms with claims that Iranian missiles struck and destroyed Pakistan-flagged oil tankers while they were transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
Pro-BJP X handle Jaipur Dialogues (@JaipurDialogues), which is run by retired IAS officer Sanjay Dixit, posted the clip on March 27, asserting that it depicted the aftermath of an Iranian missile strike on a Pakistan-flagged oil tanker “just a few hours ago.” (Archive)
A Pakistan flagged oil tanker was destroyed by Iranian missiles few hours earlier while crossing strait of Hormuz😂
— The Jaipur Dialogues (@JaipurDialogues) March 27, 2026
The post garnered around 264,000 views. Readers should note that X user @JaipurDialogues has been found sharing misinformation and propaganda several times in the past.
Another X account, The Unknown Man (@Theunk5555), shared the video with the same claim. (Archive)
A Pakistan flagged oil tanker was destroyed by Iranian missiles few hours earlier while crossing strait of Hormuz.
Pakistan has earlier betrayed Iran by re-routing Iranian oil to US.
This is a reply by Iran to Pakistan pic.twitter.com/VnBUVANVJn— THE UNKNOWN MAN (@Theunk5555) March 27, 2026
The post amassed nearly 4 million views.
Dhruv Wadhwa (@dhruvwadhwa), co-convenor of media management and media relations, BJP Punjab, did the same. (Archive)
Pakistani flagged oil tanker after crossing Strait of Hornouz without permission destroyed by Iranian missiles.
Reply to Pakistan after betraying Iran by putting its flag on US Oil Vessels.
Isko vichola banna tha 🤣🤣
#FuelStabilityUnderModi #Irán #Pakistan… pic.twitter.com/KC16UVj7bl
— Dhruv Wadhwa (@dhruvwadhwa) March 27, 2026
Several other social media users made similar claims by sharing the same video. A few of them are shown in the gallery below.
Fact Check
Upon a closer examination of the footage, we could read part of the vessel’s name, which was “Safeen”.
Subsequently, a relevant keyword search led us to a maritime tracking website, MarineTraffic, that indicated the ship in question was a container ship named ‘Safeen Prestige ‘, built in 2013 and sailing under the flag of Malta.
Based on data from marine tracking platforms like MarineTraffic, container ship Safeen Prestige was last reported in the Oman Gulf 23 days and 8 hours ago.
In addition, Vesselfinder.com indicated that the vessel was last located in the Persian Gulf 24 days ago.
Safeen Feeders is a UAE-based concern that provides regional and international trade route connectivity across container feedering and dry bulk shipping.
Further corroboration comes from a Reuters report from March 4, 2026, which documented an incident involving a Malta-flagged container ship struck by an unidentified projectile while navigating toward the Strait of Hormuz.
According to the report, the vessel was hit approximately two nautical miles north of Oman, sustaining damage that caused a fire in its engine room. A British maritime risk management firm, Vanguard, told Reuters that the projectile struck just above the waterline, but no environmental damage was reported.
A visual comparison between the vessel in the viral video and images of ‘Safeen Prestige’ present in the MarineTraffic website’s gallery reveals strong similarities, confirming the identification.
To sum up, the viral footage appears to show a Malta-flagged container ship that was struck earlier in March by an unidentified projectile. The claim that Iranian missiles targeted and destroyed Pakistan-flagged oil tankers is false and misleading.
claim_text: A video showing an Iranian missile struck and destroyed Pakistan-flagged oil tankers while they were transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
original_post_text: A Pakistan flagged oil tanker was destroyed by Iranian missiles few hours earlier while crossing strait of Hormuz😂 ||| A Pakistan flagged oil tanker was destroyed by Iranian missiles few hours earlier while crossing strait of Hormuz. Pakistan has earlier betrayed Iran by re-routing Iranian oil to US. This is a reply by Iran to Pakistan pic.twitter.com/VnBUVANVJn ||| #BREAKING :

title: BJP shares clipped video of Mamata’s speech to claim she ‘mocked demolition of temples’
body_text:
With the high-stakes Bengal elections weeks away, BJP West Bengal on March 28 shared a 8-second video of a pre-election speech by West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee. The party claimed on X that Banerjee had said BJP did much drama if a Shiv Mandir was destroyed, which implied that demolition of Hindu temples was not an important issue to her.
“Under her regime, Hindus have been repeatedly targeted- Durga Puja has been stopped, Saraswati Puja has been stopped, anti Hindu riots have happened in different parts of the state,” they went on to add. (Archive)
BJP does drama if a Shiv Mandir is broken – Mamata Banerjee
For Mamata Banerjee, Hindu temples being demolished is not an important issue. Under her regime, Hindus have been repeatedly targeted- Durga Puja has been stopped, Saraswati Puja has been stopped, anti Hindu riots have… pic.twitter.com/6NYQQfTWGU
— BJP West Bengal (@BJP4Bengal) March 28, 2026
The same video was also shared by BJP IT cell chief Amit Malviya, who peddles misinformation on a regular basis. Banerjee was “casually mocking the demolition of a temple,” he claimed. (Archive)
“If a Shiva temple is demolished, how much drama BJP creates over it!”
This is the remark by Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee, casually mocking the demolition of a temple. Earlier too, she had made deeply provocative statements about unleashing her vote bank against Hindus.
Such… pic.twitter.com/UgN0AauLci
— Amit Malviya (@amitmalviya) March 28, 2026
Among others who shared the video with the same claim are BJP leaders Pradeep Bhandari, Debjit Sarkar, Shanthi Kumar, pro-BJP X handle Trupti Garg, and Nupur J Sharma, the editor of the Right-wing propaganda website, OpIndia.
Fact Check
We scrolled through Mamata Banerjee’s Facebook timeline and found that the viral video was from a public rally held on Saturday, March 28, at Khandara football ground in Raniganj in Paschim Bardhaman district of West Bengal.
The full video of the speech can be watched here:
রানীগঞ্জের খান্দরা ফুটবল ময়দানের জনসভায় | Janasabha at Khandra Football ground, Raniganj.
রানীগঞ্জের খান্দরা ফুটবল ময়দানের জনসভায় | Janasabha at Khandra Football ground, Raniganj.
Posted by Mamata Banerjee on Friday 27 March 2026
11.17-minute mark onward in the video, Banerjee can be heard saying, “If one Shiv temple is broken, BJP does so much drama over it. Demolishing temples is not right. I do not endorse it. How many temples did BJP demolish while making the Kashi Viswanath temple?…”
It is understood that by Kashi Viswanath temple, she meant the Kashi Viswanath corridor.
So, it is clear that Banerjee did not just say that raising voice over demolition of temples was a drama, as the clipped video shared by BJP suggested. She was, in fact, pointing to what she saw as a double standard in the BJP’s approach — raising alarm in some cases while staying silent in others. Banerjee also stated that she did not support demolition of temples.
The video was clipped in a way so as to distort the statement and make it appear that she had mocked demolition of temples.
For the Kashi Viswanath Corridor, a pet project of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, around 300 structures over an area of roughly 50,000 square metre were demolished in Varanasi in 2018-19. While some locals and opposition leaders claimed that several temples were among the structures razed, the government and its agencies contested those claims. While Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath clearly stated that no temple was demolished, temple chief executive officer Vishal Singh said that ancient temples were ‘recovered’ during the demolition work.
claim_text: Mamata Banerjee mocked demolition of temples
original_post_text: BJP does drama if a Shiv Mandir is broken – Mamata Banerjee ||| “If a Shiva temple is demolished, how much drama BJP creates over it!”

title: Muslim woman on fast heckled in office? Rajasthan coaching institute video falsely viral
body_text:
A video circulating widely on social media claims to show a Muslim woman being repeatedly struck with a slipper, with the assailant mockingly saying “Good morning” between blows.
Several users have shared the clip alleging that it is from Delhi, and that a Hindu employee assaulted his Muslim colleague, who was observing a fast, to wake her up from drowsiness.
X user named @GBX_Press posted the viral clip, writing: “A fasting Muslim woman felt sleepy at work, so a Hindu manager hit her with his slipper.” (Archive)
India: A fasting Muslim woman felt sleepy at work, so a Hindu manager hit her with his slipper….😳 pic.twitter.com/fn75yJWV3i
— GBX (@GBX_Press) March 26, 2026
Another X user, @sahabnews1, also posted the viral video, claiming that it showed a Muslim woman observing a fast being harassed by her Hindu male colleague. At the time of this article being written, the post has gathered more than 2 million views, and has been re-shared on X more than 1,000 times.
Several other users on X, like (@Intl_Relations0), (@MrHoby1), (@pathan_sumaya), (@WhoAhmadRajput), also posted the video with the viral claim. 
The video was also viral on Facebook with similar claims.
For screenshots, see below:
Fact Check
To verify the authenticity of the viral claim, we broke it down into keyframes. A reverse image search on one such keyframe led us to this Instagram reel, uploaded on March 19.
The caption of this reel indicates that the incident took place in an educational institute.
Thereafter, we went through the viral clip thoroughly. On zooming in, we noticed that the watermark in the bottom-right corner of the viral clip was legible. It refers, in Hindi, to one “Commando Academy.” A text banner is situated next to the logo, which indicates two phone numbers as contacts for new admissions.
Taking cue from this, we ran a relevant keyword search, and came across the website of Commando Academy, a coaching institute based in Jaipur that guides students for admission to defence and police services.
We could ascertain that the logo of the institute matches the one attached as a watermark in the viral clip.
We also came across the Facebook profile of its founder, Veerat Choudhary. On going through his profile, we found the original video, which has recently gone viral across social media platforms. It was originally uploaded on February 13.
Similar videos of him castigating students for sleeping can be viewed here, and here.
Alt News contacted the academy and an official confirmed to us that a student was sleeping in the library and “Veerat Sir would often take rounds and wake up dozing students in that fashion.”
We also found a clarification video posted by Veerat Choudhary on the Instagram page “v.c.a.kuchamancityi” where he clarified that it was a male student whom he woke up. “He is a boy from Haryana…. He was sleeping during study hours? Should I have pampered him and woke him up gently.. saying ‘please wake up’? Had I gone soft on him, would he not have fallen asleep again…?
View this post on Instagram
In conclusion, the viral video does not show a Muslim woman on a fast being heckled in office. It is from a defense and police training institute in Jaipur and shows a teacher waking up a male student.
claim_text: Muslim woman observing fast beaten with a slipper by her Hindu manager in a government office in Delhi
original_post_text: India: A fasting Muslim woman felt sleepy at work, so a Hindu manager hit her with his slipper….😳 pic.twitter.com/fn75yJWV3i

title: Hindus celebrating Holi on Eid in Uttam Nagar? Old video from Karnataka falsely viral
body_text:
A 15-second video showing a large crowd dancing in the streets to loud music has gone viral on social media. Saffron flags can be seen across the crowd, which appears to be bathed in laser and strobe lights.
The clip was amplified after Hindutva outfits had issued statements such as “Holi shall be played with the blood of Muslims on Eid” in open threats against the Mulsim community in Delhi’s Uttam Nagar following the murder of 26-year-old Tarun Kumar on March 4, the day of Holi.
X user Vineeta Singh (@biharigurl) posted the viral clip on March 22, writing that it showed Hindus celebrating Holi in Uttam Nagar. At the time of this article being written, the video has garnered close to 5 lakh views, and has been re-shared more than 5,000 times. (Archive)
This is Uttam Nagar scenes on Eid! Well done Hindus of Uttam Nagar 🙌 pic.twitter.com/EjDUKxJnBn
— Vineeta Singh 🇮🇳 (@biharigurl) March 22, 2026
Another X user, @indiclogic, posted the clip, with the same claim. (Archive)
Tum Holi par IED lagaoge,
To
Hum #Eid par Holi manayenge.
🔥🔥🔥#Holi is Holy #EidMubarak uttamnagar !!
😎😎😎 pic.twitter.com/4EBHNUTczT— Wondering Woman 🇮🇳 (@indiclogic) March 23, 2026
The clip is also viral on Facebook and Instagram with the same claim — that it shows Hindus celebrating Holi on Eid in Uttam Nagar. A user named ‘Hindustani‘, for example, shared it on Facebook, and the post got 77,000 Likes. On Instagram, the video was shared with the same claim by several users — among them was ‘Mostly IND‘, which claims to be a ‘news and media website’. This post received 2.1 million views and 163,000 reactions. See screenshots below:
Fact Check
First, we tried to verify if the assembly of people shown in the viral video had been reported on by any media outlet. We could not find any report. We learnt that Eid was celebrated amid tight security in Delhi’s Uttam Nagar on March 21, following a Delhi High Court order to the police to ensure peace and security.
We then broke down the viral clip into keyframes. A reverse image search on one of these led us to this Instagram reel video, uploaded on September 23, 2024.
View this post on Instagram
We could ascertain that the viral clip is sourced from this video, thus proving it is old.
On inspecting the reel, we found that another user (@amar_sound_raichur) ws tagged in this reel, indicating that the video could be from Raichur, in Karnataka. Moreover, the hashtags associated with the reel denoted that it shows Ganesh Chaturthi celebrations.
At one stage of this video, we could notice a signboard on the mosque that is directly adjacent to the massive crowd dancing in the street. It says “Osmaniya Masjid”, providing us with another cue.
We searched for this mosque, adding the location of Raichur, and came across a street view of this mosque, which corroborates that the viral reel was indeed shot in this location.
A comparison between the signboards corroborates that the viral clip is not from Delhi’s Uttam Nagar, but Raichur, in Karnataka.
To sum up, we could conclude that the viral video is from Raichur, Karnataka, and was originally uploaded to Instagram in September 2024. It is being circulated with misleading communal allegations, with the claim that it shows Hindus celebrating Holi on the day of Eid, in Uttam Nagar.
claim_text: Videos shows Hindus celebrating Holi on Eid in Uttam Nagar
original_post_text: This is Uttam Nagar scenes on Eid! Well done Hindus of Uttam Nagar 🙌 pic.twitter.com/EjDUKxJnBn ||| Tum Holi par IED lagaoge, To Hum #Eid par Holi manayenge. 🔥🔥🔥 #Holi is Holy #EidMubarak uttamnagar !! 😎😎😎 pic.twitter.com/4EBHNUTczT

title: Communal disinformation: Knife-wielding man in MP temple falsely identified as a Muslim
body_text:
A video circulating on social media shows a man wielding a cleaver and threatening two others, at one point pushing and intimidating them.
The clip is being shared with claims that the assailant is a Muslim man who entered the Chintaman Ganesh Temple in Sehore, Madhya Pradesh, and carried out a “deadly attack” on the priest.
X user Oxomiya Jiyori (@SouleFacts), who has been found sharing misinformation and communal propaganda several times in the past, posted the viral clip on March 17, claiming that a Muslim person had carried out an attack on the temple priest. (Archive)
This is India’s terrified Muslim (डरा हुआ मुसलमान,) who, out of fear, entered a temple and carried out a deadly attack with a weapon on the priest…..
Incident location – Ancient Chintaman Ganesh Temple in Sehore, Madhya Pradesh….@MPPoliceDeptt Please take appropriate… pic.twitter.com/vKNIgicas6
— Oxomiya Jiyori 🇮🇳 (@SouleFacts) March 17, 2026
X user Jitendra Pratap Singh (@jpsin1), who has also been found by Alt News several times in the past spreading misinformation and communal propaganda, posted the clip with similar claims, urging the chief minister of Madhya Pradesh to be more vigilant of such occurrences. At the time of this article being written, the post has managed to accumulate more than 1.5 lakh views. (Archive)
ये है भारत का तथाकथित डरा हुआ सताया हुआ मुसलमान 🚨
मध्य प्रदेश के सीहोर के प्राचीन चिंतामन गणेश मंदिर के अंदर एक मुसलमान तलवार लेकर घुसा और मंदिर के पुजारी को जान से मारने की कोशिश किया
डॉ. मोहन यादव सो रहे हैं जबकि हिंदू मंदिरों और पुजारियों पर खुलेआम हमले हो रहे हैं 🤡 pic.twitter.com/8Yu4MffVYi
— 🇮🇳Jitendra pratap singh🇮🇳 (@jpsin1) March 17, 2026
It is pertinent to add that Singh is followed on X by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.
Fact Check
To verify whether the claims surrounding the viral clip were accurate, first, we ran a relevant keyword search to see if the incident had been reported by any reputable news agency.
We came across a news report from Bhaskar English, published on July 26, 2025, which carried the same visuals. This indicates that the incident is not a recent one.
The man seen wielding the knife has been identified as Mahesh Yadav. Reports indicate that he confronted two individuals at the temple—one of them the priest’s son—over a monetary dispute. Yadav had allegedly spent a significant amount on a case related to the priest and claimed the money was not repaid, following which he entered the temple premises with a knife to recover his dues.
A press briefing shared by the Sehore superintendent of police on X also confirms the assailant’s identity as Mahesh Yadav. He has been booked under Sections 296 and 351(2) of the BNS, along with Section 25 of the Arms Act, as stated by Sehore CSP Dr Abhinandana Sharma.
प्रेस नोट
आज दिनांक 26.07.25 को गणेश मंदिर के पुजारी को एक व्यक्ति द्वारा धारदार हथियार के साथ डराने धमकाने संबंधी वीडियो सोशल मीडिया के माध्यम से संज्ञान में आया था l पुजारी द्वारा सीसीटीवी फुटेज संलग्न कर एक आवेदन पत्र थाना मंडी में प्रस्तुत किया गया हैं जिसके आधार पर थाना मंडी… pic.twitter.com/TsiMZsGAKu— SP_SEHORE (@SpSehore) July 26, 2025
To sum up, an old video, involving a man named Mahesh Yadav threatening a temple priest with a cleaver over a monetary dispute is being falsely amplified on social media with communal tones, with allegation that the assailant was a Muslim.
claim_text: Muslim man enters Chintaman Ganesh Temple, in Madhya Pradesh and attacks priest with sharp weapon
original_post_text: This is India’s terrified Muslim (डरा हुआ मुसलमान,) who, out of fear, entered a temple and carried out a deadly attack with a weapon on the priest….. ||| ये है भारत का तथाकथित डरा हुआ सताया हुआ मुसलमान 🚨 ||| प्रेस नोट आज दिनांक 26.07.25 को गणेश मंदिर के पुजारी को एक व्यक्ति द्वारा धारदार हथियार के साथ डराने धमकाने संबंधी वीडियो सोशल मीडिया के माध्यम से संज्ञान में आया था l पुजारी द्वारा सीसीटीवी फुटेज संलग्न कर एक आवेदन पत्र थाना मंडी में प्रस्तुत किया गया हैं जिसके आधार पर थाना मंडी… pic.twitter.com/TsiMZsGAKu

title: West Asia Conflict: 2025 Syria gas station fire video falsely viral as Iran bombing Israeli city
body_text:
As tensions continue to escalate across West Asia, a video, around 33 seconds in length, is viral on social media, with the claim that it shows scenes from an Israeli city bombed with cluster missiles by Iran.
X user @bekiservet posted the viral video on March 16. The caption reads: “Even if the broadcasting ban carried a 50-year prison sentence instead of 5 years, we wouldn’t deprive people of this pleasure! You’re burning as of now, Tel Aviv. The Rishon LeZion area, where the Iranian missile fell, is burning tonight…” (Archive)
Yayın yasağının 5 yıl değil 50 yıl hapis cezası olsa bile insanları bu zevkten mahrum bırakmayız!
An itibarıyla yanıyorsun Tel Aviv🔥🔥
İran füzesinin düştüğü Rishon Le Zion bölgesi yanıyor bu gece… pic.twitter.com/w51GbG1Cb7
— Servet BEKİ (@bekiservet) March 15, 2026
Another user (@K_TURK_19051919) posted the video with similar claims. (Archive)
Konuşmanın tercümesi:
“Şu anda, İran’dan gelen bir füzenin Tel Aviv yakınlarındaki Rishon LeZion bölgesine isabet etmesinin ardından bölge bu gece alevler içinde.
Yayın yasağını ihlal etmenin cezası 5 yıl yerine 50 yıl hapis olsa bile, insanları bu andan mahrum bırakmazdık. Şu… pic.twitter.com/RGPUUdE9a2
— KABAC 𐱅𐰇𐰼𐰰 🇹🇷 (@K_TURK_19051919) March 16, 2026
Several other others on X, including @ahturgut, @BilalTekatli63, @MuammerAydin_25, @GazaNews571, also posted the viral clip, claiming that the area of Rishon LeZion, in Israel, had been bombed. (Archives: 1, 2, 3, 4)
For screenshots, see below:
Fact Check
To verify the authenticity of the claims surrounding the viral clip, first, we broke down the video into keyframes. Running a reverse image search on one such keyframe led us to this video, uploaded on Facebook, on April 23, 2025.
The caption, which is in Arabic, says: “A massive fire broke out at the ‘Al-Boushi’ gas station in the city of Al-Bab, east of Aleppo.”
Taking cue from this, we ran a relevant keyword search, and came across this news report, posted Alfajr Newspaper, which talks about the same incident.
It reports that a gas explosion in the city of Al-Bab in northern Syria left at least 28 people injuries. It took the authorities more than three hours to bring the flames under control, after it had spread to adjacent buildings.
To conclude, footage from a gas explosion in Syria in April 2025 has gone viral on social media with users incorrectly claiming that it shows an Israeli city being bombed by Irani missile clusters amid the ongoing West Asia conflict.
claim_text: The city of Rishon LeZion in Israel bombed by Iran
original_post_text: Yayın yasağının 5 yıl değil 50 yıl hapis cezası olsa bile insanları bu zevkten mahrum bırakmayız! ||| Konuşmanın tercümesi:

title: West Asia conflict: Old video falsely viral as footage from Tel Aviv after Iranian strike
body_text:
As the tension escalates in West Asia, a 20-second video showing large-scale destruction has gone viral on social media, claiming to show actual footage from Tel Aviv. The video shows an area with buildings and parked vehicles destroyed, debris scattered everywhere, and certain areas on fire.
A media agency based in Azarbaijan, named ANewZ.TV shared the purported video on Facebook, claiming that the video shows the ‘aftermath of Iranian missile strikes’ across parts of Israel, linking it to the recent conflict. (Archive)
The post has had over 7,33,000 views and 2,200 shares.
Another news outlet, Palestine International Broadcast, posted the video on Facebook, claiming that the footage shows Iranian retaliation for bombing Minab Primary School, killing 160 people, most of them school girls. (Archive)
The post has garnered over 84,000 views.
On March 10, 2026, an X user named Reem Al-Sanani (@r0_oem) shared the video claiming that according to Hebrew media, it was footage from Tel Aviv, “short while ago”. (Archive)
الإعلام العبري: قبل قليل، تل أبيب❤️🔥👇 pic.twitter.com/A4qJvPDpO8
— ريم الصنعاني (@r0_oem) March 9, 2026
Fact check
We performed a reverse image search on a few key frames of the video that led us to an X post by Iran today(@Iran) shared on June 16, 2025. This video and the now-viral clip are exactly the same.
Footage shows the extent of destruction in Tel Aviv as a result of #Iran‘s missile strike#IranUnderAttack #Israel pic.twitter.com/o7xDl5CZag
— Iran’s Today (@Iran) June 16, 2025
We found another post with the same visuals on Facebook by an Arabic News website, Jordan News. It was also posted on June 16, 2025. (Archive)
دمار هائل في تل أبيب وحيفا صباح اليوم
Posted by صحيفة أخبار الأردن on Sunday 15 June 2025
Hostility between Iran and Israel dates back to the late 1970s but their confrontation was largely a shadow war involving proxy groups across the region. Tensions escalated in April 2024, when Iran launched its first direct missile and drone attack on Israel following an Israeli strike on an Iranian diplomatic facility in Damascus. The conflict entered a more direct phase in 2025, when Israel carried out major strikes on Iranian military and nuclear sites, triggering a short but intense exchange of attacks between the two countries.
To sum up, the viral video circulating online does not depict recent footage from Tel Aviv, and is not related to the ongoing conflict in West Asia. It has been available on the internet since at least June 2025.
claim_text: Video shows Iranian missile strike in Tel Aviv
original_post_text: الإعلام العبري: قبل قليل، تل أبيب❤️‍🔥👇 pic.twitter.com/A4qJvPDpO8 ||| Footage shows the extent of destruction in Tel Aviv as a result of #Iran ‘s missile strike #IranUnderAttack #Israel pic.twitter.com/o7xDl5CZag

title: Israelis smashing street cams to hide missile strike footage? Old, unrelated video falsely viral
body_text:
Amid the escalating conflict in West Asia, a video has been circulating widely on social media, showing men in uniform smashing and removing cameras mounted on poles and buildings. It is claimed that Israelis are taking down these cameras to hide footage of Iranian attacks and the destruction caused by Iran.
The video contains two separate clips. In the first clip, a man wearing a uniform with an Israeli badge is seen using a shovel to smash a CCTV camera. In the second part of the video, another man wearing a similar uniform is standing on a ladder and removing a CCTV camera.
The video is circulating on multiple social media platforms.
An X handle named MintPress News (@MintPressNews) with over 2.5 lakh followers shared the video with the claim that the Israelis were smashing street cameras to hide the Iranian Missile Strikes footage. (Archive)
🚨Israelis Are Smashing Street Cameras To Hide Footage Of Iranian Missile Strikes
As part of its unprecedented campaign of censorship, the Israeli authorities are even destroying street cameras and CCTV.
This is on top of threatening their citizens with jail time & fines. pic.twitter.com/7h7fzfUnhs
— MintPress News (@MintPressNews) March 9, 2026
The post gained more than 145,000 views.
A Facebook page with over 1.5 lakh followers, named Chinasa Nworu, posted the video with a similar claim. The post managed to garner over 649,000 views. (Archive)
An account on Instagram, TC MEDIA (tcmedianews) shared the same video on March 9, 2026, with a similar claim while citing Tasnim News Agency as their source of information. (Archive)
View this post on Instagram
Fact Check
We performed reverse image search on a few key frames of the viral video, which led us to an Instagram post by Western Galilee Police (police_galilmaravi) posted on October 22, 2025, with a caption, which, when translated into English, reads “Governance and sovereignty to the residents. Policemen of the Western Galilee Station continue to dismantle cameras installed by criminal organisations and weaknesses in the public space.” (Archive)
View this post on Instagram
As readers can see, the video in this Instagram post contains both the shorter clips that are part of the now-viral video.
Following this, we conducted a keyword search that led us to various reports from Israeli media. One local news outlet reported that the police in Western Galilee dismantled approximately 19 cameras which were suspected to have been installed by criminals for surveillance and other illegal activities.
Additionally, a report from Haifa News on March 28, 2025, stated that the coast district police from the Haifa station had removed around 20 security cameras that Israeli authorities claimed were pirated and installed by criminal elements. These cameras were pointed at public spaces in the city.
To sum up, the viral video claiming that Israelis were smashing street cameras to hide footage of Iranian missile strikes is misleading. The video is not recent, and the CCTV cameras were actually being removed by local police because they had been installed by criminal organisations.
claim_text: Video shows Israeli men dismantling cameras from public spaces to to hide footage of Iranian attacks and the destruction caused by Iran.
original_post_text: 🚨Israelis Are Smashing Street Cameras To Hide Footage Of Iranian Missile Strikes

title: Footage of Uttam Nagar fish market fire viral as explosion in India-Israel joint defence facility in Delhi
body_text:
A 22-second video of a massive fire is being widely shared on social media with claims that it shows a blaze at a joint India–Israel defence facility in Delhi. Users further allege that Indian media is blaming Iran or pro-Iran groups for the incident.
An X user, @llliiua_, posted the clip on March 12 with a caption in Arabic claiming: “Urgent: A massive fire broke out in a joint India-Israel defense facility in Delhi, resulting in the deaths of several workers from both countries. Indian media is blaming supporters of Iran in India.”
The post has garnered over 5.48 lakh views and has been reposted more than 500 times, amplifying the unverified claim. (Archive)
عاجل:
اندلع حريق هائل في منشأة دفاعية مشتركة بين الهند وإسرائيل في دلهي، ما أسفر عن مقتل عدد من العمال من كلا البلدين.
وتُلقي وسائل الإعلام الهندية باللوم على مؤيدي إيران في الهند.pic.twitter.com/JYDTuDCzpF
— العالم الآن | WORLD NOW (@llliiua_) March 11, 2026
Another X account, @IBN_Now, shared the same clip on March 12, claiming in the caption: “BREAKING. New Delhi, India — a fire broke out at a drone manufacturing factory jointly run by Israel and India. Explosions and flames erupted everywhere, with multiple Indian and Israeli casualties reported.”
The post has received over 20,00 views, further amplifying the claim on social media. (Archive)
🚨 BREAKING
New Delhi, India — a fire broke out at a drone manufacturing factory shared by Israel and India.
Explosions and flames erupted everywhere, with multiple Indian and Israeli casualties reported. pic.twitter.com/Xh9jyKzDdW— Iran Breaking News (@IBN_Now) March 12, 2026
Several other pages and users posted the same clip, claiming that the video showed an explosion in a joint India-Israel defence facility in New Delhi. Below are a few instances.
Fact Check
We noticed that @MEAFactCheck, the Union ministry of external affairs’ fact check account on X, had described the post as ‘fake news’. There was no further details.
Fake News Alert!
This is Fake News.
Please stay alert against such false and baseless claims on social media! pic.twitter.com/vtqwmdj4nQ
— MEA FactCheck (@MEAFactCheck) March 12, 2026
To find more information, we broke down the video into several keyframes and ran a reverse image search on some of them. This led us to a news report by NDTV India titled, “A massive fire broke out in the fish market in Uttam Nagar, Delhi, burning several shops and huts”. The report was published on March 12 and carried a screengrab of the now-viral video. It mentioned that the fire broke out in the fish market area of Uttam Nagar in the early hours of March 12. “Approximately 300 to 400 huts were destroyed in a fire that broke out last night in Delhi’s Uttam Nagar area. The cause of the fire is still unclear”.
We also came across an X post by the news agency, Press Trust of India. The post was shared on March 12 at 12:53 am. It carried the viral video and the caption mentioned that a fire broke out in Uttam Nagar, Delhi, at the Machhli Mandi.
VIDEO | Delhi: Massive fire breaks out in Uttam Nagar near Machhli Mandi. Firefighting operations underway. More details are awaited.
(Source: Third Party)
(Full video available on PTI Videos – https://t.co/n147TvrpG7) pic.twitter.com/R2PyMw7iLh
— Press Trust of India (@PTI_News) March 11, 2026
Hence, from the above findings, it is clear that the viral video does not depict an explosion in a joint India-Israel defence facility. It is footage of a fire that broke out in the market area of Uttam Nagar in Delhi.
claim_text: Video shows India-Israel joint defence facility on fire in Delhi
original_post_text: عاجل: ||| 🚨 BREAKING ||| Fake News Alert! ||| VIDEO | Delhi: Massive fire breaks out in Uttam Nagar near Machhli Mandi. Firefighting operations underway. More details are awaited.

title: Netanyahu’s brother killed in Iranian missile strike at his home? Unrelated video from US falsely viral
body_text:
A video showing a mansion engulfed on fire is going viral on social media. The entire building is up in flames, and firefighters are present at the scene. The video is being shared in connection to the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict. Posts claim that Iran directly attacked Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s home with a ballistic missile, burning his brother, Iddo Netanyahu, to death.
A user named ‘Hakan Boyav’ posted a viral video on the social media platform X, writing that the end has come for Netanyahu, the child-killer. An Iranian ballistic missile directly hit Netanyahu’s home, burning Netanyahu’s brother, Iddo Netanyahu, to death. (Archive)
Çocuk katili Netanyahu’nun sonu gelmiştir.
Netanyahu’nun kardeşi Iddo Netanyahu yanarak can vermiş.
Evinde diri diri yakıldı. İran harika bir iş çıkardı. Netanyahu’nun evine doğrudan balistik füzeyle leş oldu.
Eline sağlık İran. pic.twitter.com/WN1Z1k7OFv
— Hakan Boyav 🇹🇷 (@hakannbyv) March 9, 2026
A user named Waqas Mughal shared the viral video, claiming that Benjamin Netanyahu’s brother, Ado Netanyahu, died after an Iranian missile attack on his family’s home. (Archive)
A user named ‘La Catrina Norteña’ shared the viral video stating that Iranian media outlets had reported how Netanyahu’s brother’s house was set on fire, resulting in his death. (Archive link)
Fact Check
Alt News performed a reverse image search with frames from the viral video and found a report from BreakingAC, a US local news website, dated February 10, 2026. The article featured a photo similar to the viral video. According to the report, the incident actually occurred in Galloway Township, New Jersey, USA, where a massive fire broke out in a house in the intervening night of February 8 and 9.
The report clearly states that, according to Galloway Township police, firefighters had a difficult time extinguishing the fire due to the extreme cold, snow, and frost. The homeowner immediately called for help after noticing smoke and sounding the alarm. Fortunately, everyone inside the home managed to get out safely, and there were no reports of injuries or deaths.
A Facebook post has been embedded in this article. When we checked the Facebook post, we found that the viral video was posted on February 9. The address of the incident was given as ‘Park Place, Galloway, New Jersey’.
When we searched the address mentioned in the Facebook post on Google Street View, we saw the same bungalow which is seen burning in the viral video.
This proves that the incident of fire was reported in New Jersey, USA, and has no connection to the Iran-Israel war or Netanyahu’s family. Claims on social media that the alleged video shows a fire at Netanyahu’s brother’s home which resulted in his death are baseless and false.
claim_text: A video showing Netanyahu's brother's home on fire leading to his death
original_post_text: Çocuk katili Netanyahu’nun sonu gelmiştir.

title: Times Now, ABP, Zee, Republic misreport 2020 Beirut video as Iranian missile strike in Dubai
body_text:
A 25-second video is going viral on social media showing a woman livestreaming when she is suddenly interrupted by the sound of a loud explosion. Moments later, a blast wave appears to hit her room, toppling both her and her recording device. The screen then goes completely blank.
The video was posted on X by Times Now with the caption: “Viral video shows a woman livestreaming from her Dubai apartment moments before it was struck by an Iranian missile, causing massive destruction.”
The post, shared on March 2, 2026, received over 169,000 views and 159 reposts at the time of this article being written. (Archive)
Viral video shows a woman livestreaming from her Dubai apartment moments before it was struck by an Iranian missile, causing massive destruction. pic.twitter.com/hox49uBLsn
— TIMES NOW (@TimesNow) March 2, 2026
Other media outlets such as Lokmat Times, NewsX and Republic also posted the same video with similar caption. They described the video as showing an incident in Dubai.
News portals such as Zee News Marathi (Archive) and ABP News Hindi (Archive) published articles based on the same video, echoing the same claim.
On February 28, the United States and Israel launched a joint assault on Iran, targeting key military installations. The strikes reportedly killed Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, along with several senior figures of the ruling establishment. The attacks also caused civilian casualties, including children. In retaliation, Iran carried out strikes on US military bases across Central and West Asia, including in Kuwait, the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain, Jordan and Qatar. Late on March 7, four oil depots in and around Tehran were hit killing at least six people. The situation remains fluid and highly volatile at the time of writing, with developments continuing to unfold.
The video is being shared in this context. In addition to media outlets, several social media users across various platforms have also shared the same video. Below are a few instances:
Fact Check
To verify the clip’s authenticity, we conducted a reverse image search using screenshots from the viral video. This led us to an almost six-year-old video from Beirut, which is now being reshared online as footage from Dubai.
The video was originally posted on YouTube by a Filipino migrant worker, Edlyn Candido Lacse, on August 6, 2020, with the caption: “Blast while I am on my streaming.” The original video is about one minute and 30 seconds long. (Archive)
A day earlier, on August 5, 2020, the same account had uploaded another video titled “My Beirut Explosion Experience,” further indicating that the sequence of videos was originally presented as footage from Beirut. In that clip, the scale of the devastation following the blast is clearly visible.
We also found the same interrupted livestream video uploaded by another YouTube account named Yeiron Jee, with the description: “no intended to copyright, this video was taken from live stream of my friends edlyn lacse in lebanon.” This was posted on August 4, 2020.
Beirut, the Capital city of Lebanon, was rocked by a major explosion on August 4, 2020. The explosion was caused by the ignition of around 2500 tonnes of ammonium nitrate, which was stored at the Beirut Port. The massive explosion resulted in at least 218 deaths and nearly 10 thousand injuries. The video in question had captured the whole essence of this devastating incident.
Further, when the 2020 video began resurfacing online as an incident from Dubai, Filipino politician and journalist Raffy Tulfo issued a clarifying video addressing the claims. He also conducted an online interview with Edlyn Candido Lacse, who recounted her harrowing experience of surviving the Beirut explosion while livestreaming on August 4, 2020.
The full interaction is available on his official Facebook page, Raffy Tulfo in Action.
Hence, from the above findings, it is clear that the viral clip does not depict the consequences of an Iranian strike in Dubai, nor did it emerge during the ongoing conflict in West Asia. It originated way back, in August, 2020 on the day of the Beirut port explosion.
claim_text: Video shows woman's livestream disrupted by Iranian missile strike in Dubai
original_post_text: Viral video shows a woman livestreaming from her Dubai apartment moments before it was struck by an Iranian missile, causing massive destruction. pic.twitter.com/hox49uBLsn

title: No, Bahrain has not arrested an Indian national for espionage
body_text:
Amid escalating tensions in West Asia, several social media users have claimed that Bahrain has arrested an Indian national named Nitin Mohan on charges of espionage for Mossad, the Israeli intelligence service. The claim is accompanied by an image of a bearded man in a black T-shirt, handcuffed in front of a backdrop featuring the emblem of Bahrain’s interior ministry.
A Pakistan-based X account, The Intel Consortium, shared the purported image, claiming that an Indian telecommunications engineer, Nitin Mohan, had been arrested by Bahrain’s intelligence for “transmitting sensitive geospatial data, photographs, and video reconnaissance of strategic locations” to Mossad. (Archive)
The post garnered around 1.2 million views.
Another X account, Artaban 1727 AD (@3lm_a1), shared the same image with similar claims, stating that an Indian national named Nitin Mohan had been arrested by Bahraini authorities on charges of leaking sensitive data to Israel’s Mossad agency. (Archive)
🚨 عاجل :
السلطات البحرينية تعتقل مهندس اتصالات هندي يُدعى نيتين موهان بتهمة تسريب بيانات حساسة إلى جهاز الموساد الإسرائيلي.
▫️المتهم قام بحسب التقارير الأولية ، بنقل بيانات جغرافية وصور وفيديوهات استطلاع لمواقع استراتيجية داخل البحرين.
▫️المعلومات المسربة تتعلق بمواقع حساسة… pic.twitter.com/EglMzYIay7— أرطبون ﮼١٧٢٧م ⚖️🇸🇦 (@3lm_a1) March 10, 2026
The post amassed around 1 million views.
Additionally, another Pakistan-based X-account, Expose Propaganda (@EPropoganda1), shared the purported image on March 10, 2026, with similar claims. (Archive)
Pakistani newspaper Minute Mirror published a story on its web portal on March 10, headlined “Bahrain arrests Indian national allegedly spying for Israeli Mossad”. The report carried the same photo.
Several social media users have amplified the same claim with the purported image. A few of them are shown in the gallery below:
Fact Check
During our investigation, we found a press release issued by Bahrain’s interior ministry on March 9, 2026. The release stated that six Asian nationals had been arrested by the cybercrime department of the General Directorate for Anti-Corruption and Economic and Electronic Security.
They were identified as Mohammad Muar Akbar, Afzal Khan, Ahmad Mumtaz, Arslan Ali Sajid and Abdul Rahman Abdul Sattar from Pakistan and Muhammad Israfil Mir from Bangladesh.
According to the statement, the individuals were referred to public prosecution for “filming, publishing, and republishing video clips” related to the impacts of Iranian aggression. They were accused of sympathising with and glorifying acts of hostility in a way that could undermine public order. The authorities also said that the videos were shared through their social media accounts, contributing to the spread of misinformation and fear among citizens and residents.
The press release does not mention any Indian national, nor does it reference any acts of espionage related to Mossad. Also, the individual shown in the viral photo does not feature among the six.
When we performed a reverse image search with the viral image, we found that it had no earlier matches. This strongly suggests that the image is artificially generated.
To sum up, the claim that an Indian national named Nitin Mohan has been arrested by the Bahrain authorities under the charges of spying for Mossad is baseless and false. Six Asians — including five Pakistanis and one Bangladeshi — were arrested for alleged cybercrime violations in Bahrain.
claim_text: Bahrain authorities arrested Indian national Nitin Mohan on charges of spying for Mossad, Israel's intelligence
original_post_text: 🚨 عاجل :

title: Photo shown by Trinamool to claim Modi disrespected President Murmu is misleading
body_text:
Reacting to the the BJP’s charge of protocol lapses during President Droupadi Murmu’s visit to north Bengal, the Trinamool Congress on March 10 produced a 2024 photo showing Murmu standing while L K Advani and Prime Minister Narendra Modi were seated in a room, and claimed that it was Prime Minister Narendra Modi who had disrespected the President by remaining seated.
President Murmu visited north Bengal to attend a Santal tribal conference on March 7. After the event, she publicly expressed displeasure that neither the chief minister nor any state minister was present to receive her during the visit. There was also controversy over the venue change of the conference, which the President reportedly said had been altered without proper coordination. While Banerjee denied any protocol breach, leaders from the BJP accused the West Bengal government of disrespecting the President and violating protocol.
Prime Minister Narendra Modi lashed out at Mamata Banerjee in an X post. “The anguish and concern expressed by the President, who has risen from the tribal community itself, have instilled profound sorrow in the hearts of the people of India”, the PM wrote.
The Trinamool Congress responded to this on Monday, March 10.
The photo in question, printed as a large banner, was held up on Monday by TMC leaders at Banerjee’s ongoing sit-in protest against the SIR exercise at the Metro Channel in Esplanade. Banerjee said, “Honourable PM, this is for you. Do you respect the President who is a woman and hails from the tribal community? Then tell us why is the President standing while you remain seated? I am showing this to you clearly. We show our respect. But they do not. This photo proves who can show respect and who can’t…”
Trinamool MP and former journalist Sagarika Ghose also called out Prime Minister Modi for disrespecting the President.
“Why was she not allowed to sit in a chair?”, Sagarika Ghose wrote, circulating the same photo, which showed the PM seated in the company of L K Advani, with Droupadi Murmu standing next to them in the same frame. (Archive)
“Mr Prime Minister @narendramodi is this how you respect @rashtrapatibhvn ? You remain seated while Draupadi Murmuji has to stand? Why don’t you stand up for the President of India, Modiji? Why was she not allowed to sit in a chair? Why do you keep abusing Bengal just before… pic.twitter.com/gyvC1gvCD2
— Sagarika Ghose (@sagarikaghose) March 8, 2026
The Trinamool Congress also shared a video from its official X handle and claimed that “when it comes to the Hon’ble President of India, Mr. Modi cannot even extend the most basic courtesy of standing up in her presence.”
Who says @narendramodi doesn’t respect a President? He is always impeccably respectful when it comes to the President of the United States, for whose benefit he has sung and danced in Israel, at whose beck and call he appears ready to make strategic, diplomatic and even military… pic.twitter.com/1LTSTWaAr8
— All India Trinamool Congress (@AITCofficial) March 8, 2026
Fact Check
We ran a reverse image search on the concerned photo and found that it was taken on March 31, 2024, when BJP leader L K Advani was conferred the Bharat Ratna. The ceremony was held at his residence due to his poor health and was attended by several senior leaders, including Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Droupadi Murmu.
We then carried out relevant keyword searches and found a video shared on the verified YouTube channel of Narendra Modi on March 31, 2024, which shows moments from the same ceremony.
In this video, it is clearly seen that the Indian President is initially seated in a chair right beside PM Modi and Advani. She only stands up to award the latter with the Bharata Ratna medallion and award. Other dignitaries present in the room, including then vice-president Jagdeep Dhankhar and former VP Venkaiah Naidu, are also seen seated.
President Murmu herself had posted on X about the ceremony.
एक सांसद के रूप में संवाद पर उनकी आस्था ने संसदीय परंपराओं को समृद्ध किया। गृह मंत्री तथा उप प्रधान मंत्री के रूप में, उन्होंने सदैव राष्ट्र हित को सर्वोपरि रखा, जिससे उन्हें दलगत सीमाओं से परे जा कर लोगों ने सम्मान और प्रशंसा प्रदान की। भारत के सांस्कृतिक पुनरुत्थान के लिए उनके… pic.twitter.com/e4QYXQt0tX
— President of India (@rashtrapatibhvn) March 31, 2024
In one of the photos, she can be seen seated alongside PM Modi and L K Advani.
To sum up, while it is true that the Prime Minister did not get up from his chair when the President stood up to confer the award to Advani — and it was this moment that was captured in the photo amplified by the Trinamool — it is also true that there was a chair designated for President Murmu and she was seated there during the ceremony.
Trinamool MP Sagarika Ghose’s remark on X that the President was not allowed to sit in a chair is completely baseless and false.
claim_text: PM Modi remained seated while President Draupadi Murmu was standing

Why don’t you stand up for the President of India, Modiji? Why was she not allowed to sit in a chair? Why do you keep abusing Bengal just before… pic.twitter.com/gyvC1gvCD2 ||| Who says @narendramodi doesn’t respect a President? He is always impeccably respectful when it comes to the President of the United States, for whose benefit he has sung and danced in Israel, at whose beck and call he appears ready to make strategic, diplomatic and even military… pic.twitter.com/1LTSTWaAr8 ||| एक सांसद के रूप में संवाद पर उनकी आस्था ने संसदीय परंपराओं को समृद्ध किया। गृह मंत्री तथा उप प्रधान मंत्री के रूप में, उन्होंने सदैव राष्ट्र हित को सर्वोपरि रखा, जिससे उन्हें दलगत सीमाओं से परे जा कर लोगों ने सम्मान और प्रशंसा प्रदान की। भारत के सांस्कृतिक पुनरुत्थान के लिए उनके… pic.twitter.com/e4QYXQt0tX

title: Viral clip of Army Chief Upendra Dwivedi ‘admitting’ India gave away Iranian ship’s location is a deepfake
body_text:
A 57-second video of India’s Chief of Army Staff Upendra Dwivedi is viral on social media. Users are sharing the clip with the claim that Dwivedi “admitted” that India had “betrayed” Iran by sharing the location of an Iranian naval ship with Israel.
The video is circulating amid the ongoing war in West Asia with United States and Israel on one side and Iran on the other. On March 4, the Iranian naval vessel IRIS Dena — which was stationed in the Indian Ocean with around 130 personnel on board after participating in a naval event in Visakhapatnam — was reportedly torpedoed by a US submarine near the southern coast of Sri Lanka. The warship was returning to Iran after taking part in the multinational naval exercise hosted by India.
In the viral video, the COAS says, “We are closely monitoring the situation in Iran. Israel is our close ally. The Iranian naval ship being sunk is indeed unfortunate, but it was done in international waters. As long as the Iranian ship remained in Indian waters, they remained protected, but when they crossed into international waters, as Israeli strategic allies, it was our duty to inform Israel of their exact location as a part of our newer strategic deal. So, you’re wrong to assume that Iranian naval ships were destroyed within Indian territory. What Israel does in international waters is their business. We can provide Israel with location or intelligence, but we had no part in the Israeli and US joint torpedo attack. They did their business; it is Iran’s war, and India has nothing to do with it. Our focus remains on Pakistan, and Israel will help us achieve that goal. We remain grateful to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for helping us seek permission to import Russian fuel.”
X page GPX (@GPX_Press) posted the viral video on March 9, claiming, “India confesses it BETRAYED Iran by leaking the location of an Iranian ship to Israel, leading to its total destruction!…”
The post has received more than 43,500 views and has been reshared over 400 times. (Archive)
Another X page geopol•pt (@GeopolPt) also shared the viral clip on March 9, claiming the same. The post has received over 32,000 views and has been reshared over 100 times.
Several other users posted the viral clip claiming that India had “betrayed” Iran by sharing the Iranian naval ship’s location with Israel.
Alt News also received requests on itsWhatsApp helpline to verify the authenticity of this video.
Fact Check
We noticed a ticker in the viral clip reading “Raisina Dialogue 2026 × Firstpost.”
Using this clue, we ran a keyword search on YouTube and found a video published by Firstpost on March 7 titled “India’s Army Chief Speaks on Op Sindoor, Pakistan and Future of Warfare | Raisina Dialogue 2026.”
In the 21-minute interview, Upendra Dwivedi is in conversation with Harsh V. Pant about the lessons from Operation Sindoor and the evolving nature of warfare, as noted in the video description. This is the same video that is now viral.
At no point in the interview does Dwivedi mention the US-Israel–Iran conflict or the alleged sinking of an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean. So, it is evident that the viral clip has been lifted from this interview and edited to create a misleading narrative.
For further verification, we uploaded the viral clip to Hive Moderation, an AI detection tool. The analysis indicated a 99.9% likelihood that the speech in the clip was AI-generated.
Additionally, on March 9, the Press Information Bureau’s fact-check wing, PIB Fact Check, posted on X that the viral clip of COAS Dwivedi was a digitally manipulated video.
🚨 Deepfake Video Alert
Pakistani propaganda accounts are sharing a digitally manipulated video of the Indian Army Chief General Upendra Dwivedi, making false claims that when the Iranian ship crossed the international waters, as Israeli strategic allies, it was our duty to… pic.twitter.com/itQ63pXGuF
— PIB Fact Check (@PIBFactCheck) March 9, 2026
To sum up, it is clear that the viral clip does not show an actual statement made by the Indian Chief of Army Staff, Upendra Dwivedi. The video is a deepfake.
claim_text: COAS Upendra Dwivedi admits to sharing Iranian naval ship's location with Israel
original_post_text: 🚨 Deepfake Video Alert

title: Footage from Philadelphia plane crash site falsely viral as ‘confirmed’ visuals from Tel Aviv
body_text:
With tensions continuing to escalate in West Asia, an X handle named GPX (@GPX_Press) shared a video on March 8, 2026, showing a road littered with debris and what appear to be fragments of metal and other materials. Parked vehicles along the street appear damaged, while fires burn in the background amid heavy smoke.
GPX Press, which describes itself as dealing in “Global News & Politics 🌐Unfiltered updates. High-Signal, Zero-Noise”, described the video as “confirmed” footage from Tel Aviv.
At the time of this article being written, the X post has been viewed over 1 million times and reshared over 4,700 times. The video can be watched below:
It has been confirmed… the video is indeed from Tel Aviv.
Night there is no longer night… the sky has turned into a mouth of fire, and missiles multiply as if they are creatures emerging from the earth… something closer to the end of a horror movie than a news broadcast. 🔥🌪️ pic.twitter.com/TCwQXR93U2
— GPX (@GPX_Press) March 7, 2026
Footage Shows Plane Crash Site in Philadelphia
Alt News had fact-checked the same footage twice in the past.
The footage actually shows a plane crash site in Philadelphia in early 2025. According to reports, a medical transport Learjet twin-engine jet aircraft has crashed in a populated area of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania in the United States. Three buildings in the area are reportedly damaged. The aircraft was carrying six people from Mexico.
We had also geolocated the spot and independently confirmed that it was Philadelphia.
During Operation Sindoor — the military operation launched by India on May 7, 2025, involving precision strikes on alleged militant camps in Pakistan and Pakistan-administered Kashmir in the aftermath of the Pahalgam terror attack — the video was shared by some media outlets and social media users as the situation in Pakistan after the Indian armed forces reportedly hit various strategic locations in the country. Bengali news outlet ABP Ananda was among those who had falsely aired the footage.
The fact check can be read here.
In June 2025, the same video was viral again, this time as the situation in Tel Aviv after Iranian strikes. Some claimed it showed destruction by Israel in Tehran.
The fact check can be read here.
To sum up, a scene of devastation depicting a plane crash site in Philadelphia from February 2025 was falsely shared by an X handle named GPX Press as ‘confirmed’ footage from Tel Aviv.
claim_text: Footage shows confirmed footage from Tel Aviv
original_post_text: It has been confirmed… the video is indeed from Tel Aviv.

title: ‘Abu Dhabi burning’: 2022 gas cylinder blast video falsely viral amid West Asia conflict
body_text:
Amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia, a six-second video clip is circulating on social media. The footage shows an explosion on the top floor of what appears to be a residential building. It is being shared with the claim that it captures the current situation in Abu Dhabi amid the war.
An X user, Anis Mansour (@anesmansory), shared the video on March 5, 2026, claiming it shows the situation in Abu Dhabi in the context of the conflict. In his X bio, Mansour describes himself as a writer for Arabi21 News.
الان ابوظبي تحترق 🔥🔥🔥 pic.twitter.com/6yID63nVg4
— أنيس منصور (@anesmansory) March 5, 2026
The post has garnered over 200,000 views.
An X account, NewsOrbit (@NewsOrbit1122), shared the same video clip claiming that it depicted the real-time footage of Abu Dhabi. (Archive)
Now Abu Dhabi is burning 🔥🔥🔥 pic.twitter.com/EJHMmGbAXm
— NewsOrbit (@NewsOrbit1122) March 5, 2026
Another X user, Lotfi Ben Salem (@lotfi4461), shared the video with a caption, “Abu Dhabi is burning” on March 6, 2026. (Archive)
Now Abu Dhabi is burning 🔥🔥🔥#Iran / #Israel / #MiddleEast / #Lebanon / #Iraq / #Jordan / #Syria / #Yemen / #SaudiArabia / #Qatar / #UAE / #USA / #Canada / #WW3
#Minnesota #Trump #USA #MAGA#generalstrike #nationalstrike pic.twitter.com/x2EgtkCnCb— Lotfi BEN SALEM🇹🇳 (@lotfi4461) March 5, 2026
Several social media accounts have also shared this clip with similar claims. A few of them are shown in the gallery below:
Fact Check
A reverse image search on keyframes from the viral clip led us to several posts on X from four years ago, indicating that the footage predates the current conflict. One of the earliest instances we found was shared by an X account named Zero Hour (@zero_hour2020) on February 9, 2022, with the caption “Abu Dhabi now”.
We also found multiple news reports from February 2022, featuring the purported video. These reports indicate that the video was linked to a gas cylinder explosion in a residential building on Hamdan Street in Abu Dhabi, which triggered a brief fire but caused no casualties. Authorities said emergency teams quickly brought the situation under control.
According to Reuters, Abu Dhabi civil defence received a report of a fire at 12:09 am on February 9, 2022 “caused by a gas cylinder explosion in a building in a central residential area”. There were no casualties as “Specialized teams extinguished the fire, evacuated the building as a precaution and controlled the situation.”
To sum up, the viral video of the explosion does not depict the current situation in Abu Dhabi amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia. The footage dates back to February 9, 2022, and shows a gas cylinder explosion in a residential area. Claims linking the video to the present conflict are false and misleading.
claim_text: a video showing an explosion in a building in Abu Dhabi amidst recent west asia conflict

title: US–Israel strikes in Iran: Old video of Mahmoud Ahmadinejad falsely shared as evidence of him being alive
body_text:
A video featuring Mahmoud Ahmadinejad is going viral on social media, with users sharing the clip as purported proof that Ahmadinejad is alive. The claim is circulating in the backdrop of the ongoing conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the United States, during which Israel has reportedly targeted and claimed to have eliminated several prominent members of the Iranian establishment, including the country’s supreme leader Ali Khamenei and Ahmadinejad.
An X user, @Sentletse, posted the video on March 4, claiming in the caption that Ahmadinejad is “alive and well.” The post has garnered over 501,000 views and has been reposted more than 1,200 times. (Archive)
Mahmoud Ahmadinejad is alive and well.
The President that Iran needs right now. pic.twitter.com/ypubwH7upF
— Sentletse 🇿🇦🇷🇺🇵🇸🇱🇧 (@Sentletse) March 4, 2026
News outlet Zee News published a report on March 4 that included a screengrab from the viral video. The headline of the report, originally in Hindi, translates roughly to: “Is the Tiger Really Still Alive? Iran’s top leader appears after 4 days, evading the US attack.”
The article used the screengrab from the viral clip while suggesting that Ahmadinejad had resurfaced days after reports of Israeli strikes targeting members of Iran’s leadership. (Archive)
On march 4, Azerbaijani media outlet Azernews published an article citing the viral video and claimed that Ahmadinejad was seen alive. The headline said, ‘Former Iran President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad appears in video after death rumors circulate’.
Several other users shared the same video, claiming that it proved that the former Iranian president was alive.
Fact Check
To verify the authenticity of the claim, we first checked whether the video was recent. We broke the viral clip into multiple keyframes and ran a reverse image search on several of them. This led us to an Instagram post dated February 15, 2026, in which a user had shared the same video that is now circulating online.
The caption of the post, written in Persian, translates to:
*“Presence of Dr. Ahmadinejad in Shirgah, Savadkuh, Mazandaran Province. January 18, 2026.
At the funeral and burial ceremony of the respected mother of Dr. Seyed Hassan Mousavi, the Chief of Staff to the President during the 10th government. People of Savadkuh expressed their affection toward Dr. Ahmadinejad.”*
From the caption, it is clear that the video predates the current conflict. It was filmed on January 18, 2026, when Mahmoud Ahmadinejad attended a funeral ceremony in Shirgah, located in Mazandaran Province. This shows that the clip is not recent and is unrelated to the ongoing hostilities involving Iran, Israel, and the United States.
View this post on Instagram
On March 1, The Jerusalem Post reported that Mahmoud Ahmadinejad had been killed in US–Israel strikes, attributing the claim to the Iranian Labour News Agency. However, on March 3, Iran International reported that Ahmadinejad was alive, citing unnamed “sources”.
At present, there is no conclusive confirmation regarding Ahmadinejad’s status, as multiple Iranian media outlets have published conflicting reports on the matter. What can be established, however, is that the viral video is not recent. Our verification shows that the clip dates back to January 18, 2026, when Ahmadinejad attended a funeral ceremony in northern Iran.
claim_text: Viral clip shows former Iranian president Mahmoud Ahmadinejad is alive after assassination attempt
original_post_text: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad is alive and well.

title: Old video of man shooting himself falsely shared as Iranian general who ‘betrayed’ Khamenei
body_text:
Trigger Warning: Suicide. Viewer Discretion is Advised.
A 25-second video circulating on social media shows a man briefly addressing the camera before shooting himself in the head with a pistol. Social media users claim that the clip shows an Iranian general who allegedly leaked the coordinates of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, enabling US and Israeli forces to assassinate him. According to these claims, the officer took his own life to evade capture and execution by the Iranian state.
Israel and the United States launched a coordinated military offensive against Iran on February 28, 2026, targeting multiple military and government facilities across the country. The opening wave of strikes reportedly killed Khamenei along with several senior Iranian officials and military commanders.
The video has been widely shared on WhatsApp and other social media platforms. One such post was shared on X by an account named Abu Bahar (@dwgan3) on March 4, 2026. The caption accompanying the video reads: “It’s said that this Iranian general was sending coordinates to Americans and Zionists, and after he found out that he had been exposed, he committed suicide with his personal pistol.”
At the time of this article being written, the post had garnered over 135,500 views and has been reposted 125 times.
يُقال إن هذا الجنرال الإيراني كان يرسل إحداثيات للأمريكان والصهاينة
وبعد أن عرف إنه تم كشفه
أنتحر وقتل نفسه بمسدسه الشخصي#مع_إيران_ضد_العدوان#مع_ايران_حتى_النصر pic.twitter.com/VN9LxikMKB— ((ابو بحار)) (@dwgan3) March 4, 2026
Several other X users shared the same video, echoing the same claim. Below are a few instances:
Alt News received multiple requests on its WhatsApp helpline (7600011160) to examine the authenticity of the video.
Fact Check
To verify the clip’s authenticity, we ran a reverse image search using keyframes from the video. This led us to an old post on Reddit shared on October 17, 2024, where a user under the handle r/Kurdistan had shared screenshots from the same clip with the caption: “Anyone know what the deal with him was?”
According to the Reddit thread, the viral clip is part of a 25-minute-long video in which a Kurdish man, reportedly distressed over the political environment, died by suicide during a Facebook Live broadcast. In the same thread, another user, Independent-Raise635, claimed that the man and his family had been threatened by the Kurdistan Workers’ Party (PKK), and that the harassment drove him to take the extreme step.
The Reddit post contains 21 comments, all of which are more than a year old, indicating that the video predates the current conflict and is not related to recent developments involving Iran.
Using reverse image search, we also found a post on X by an account named Rojhelat News (@NewsRojhelat), which shared snippets from the same video. The post was published on October 15, 2024, and claimed that the man seen in the clip was Zahir Amini, a resident of Baneh in eastern Kurdistan who was living in Erbil. According to the post, Amini died by suicide during an Instagram Live broadcast.
شەوی دووشەممە ٢٣ی رەزبەر هاووڵاتییەک بەناوی زاهیر ئەمینیان خەڵکی شاری بانەی رۆژهەڵاتی کوردستان دانیشتووی هەولێر لە لایڤی ئیستاگرامیدا خۆی کوشت.
وردەکاری لە ڤیدیۆگرافیکدا بخوێننەوە. pic.twitter.com/mPjVWFyfDW— Rojhelat News-فوری (@NewsRojhelat) October 15, 2024
The same video was also uploaded on the website Documenting Reality by a user named BEHEMOTH on October 18, 2024. The user had reposted the clip from another account called kellyhound.
The 58-second video was accompanied by the following caption:
“TikTok witnessed a tragic incident when a Kurdish citizen broadcasted live to express his severe suffering and harsh conditions. While speaking, he said: ‘The party and the Yekti destroyed us, and the government of the two families did to us what the enemies did not do,’ referring to the economic and social pressures he is experiencing. The citizen spoke about the difficulty of raising his children and providing for their needs, which increased his feelings of frustration and despair. In a painful moment, the citizen hugged his son before he committed suicide in front of the live broadcast camera.”
Hence, from the above findings it is clear that the viral clip is unrelated to the ongoing conflict in West Asia and has been available on the internet for over a year. It has no connection with the reported killing of Iranian leader Ayatollah Khamenei.
claim_text: Video shows Iranian general who leaked the coordinates of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei killing himself
original_post_text: يُقال إن هذا الجنرال الإيراني كان يرسل إحداثيات للأمريكان والصهاينة وبعد أن عرف إنه تم كشفه أنتحر وقتل نفسه بمسدسه الشخصي #مع_إيران_ضد_العدوان #مع_ايران_حتى_النصر pic.twitter.com/VN9LxikMKB ||| شەوی دووشەممە ٢٣ی رەزبەر هاووڵاتییەک بەناوی زاهیر ئەمینیان خەڵکی شاری بانەی رۆژهەڵاتی کوردستان دانیشتووی هەولێر لە لایڤی ئیستاگرامیدا خۆی کوشت. وردەکاری لە ڤیدیۆگرافیکدا بخوێننەوە. pic.twitter.com/mPjVWFyfDW

title: Mass burial of students in Iran: Haunting photos mired in false fact-check muddle
body_text:
The US–Israel joint strikes on Iran, which began on February 28, have reportedly taken a heavy civilian toll, including a devastating attack on the Shajareh Tayyebeh school in Minab in southern Iran. Iranian state media reported that at least 168 people — most of them schoolchildren — were killed in the bombing, while nearly 100 others were injured.
A report by The Guardian painted a picture of the destruction: “Photographs and verified videos from the site…show children’s bodies lying partly buried under the debris. In one video, a very small child’s severed arm is pulled from the rubble.” The UNESCO condemned the bombing, calling it a “grave violation” of international humanitarian law.
In this context, several users, including Congress spokesperson Supriya Shrinate, posted an aerial photo showing proceedings of a mass burial, with graves being dug by bulldozers.
Shrinate wrote that graves were being dug to bury schoolgirls killed in US-Israel bombing.
Devastating
165 graves being dug for the girls killed by US-Israel bombing on their school
This was done by US-Israel in the name of empowering women
📷 Iran Foreign Minister pic.twitter.com/Y8SfXjndIk
— Supriya Shrinate (@SupriyaShrinate) March 3, 2026
Pro-BJP influencer and Supreme Court lawyer Shashank Shekhar Jha (@shashank_ssj) was quick to call out this post, claiming that the photo of the mass grave used by Supriya Shrinate was an old photograph showing COVID-stricken Indonesia. (Archive)
This picture is of Indonesia during Covid 🤷🏽♂️ https://t.co/ekIc5FxK6m
— Shashank Shekhar Jha (@shashank_ssj) March 3, 2026
At the time of this article being written, Shashank Jha’s post which sought to debunk the claims of the photo being shot in Iran, has been viewed more than 6 lakh times, and has been re-shared more than 3,000 times.
On March 4, Jha again ‘fact-checked’ another photo depicting the same burial site, this time replying to an X post by journalist Rajdeep Sardesai. (Archive)
This picture is of Indonesia during Covid 🤷🏽♂️ https://t.co/nTKnKEesug
— Shashank Shekhar Jha (@shashank_ssj) March 4, 2026
Fact Check
On running a reverse image search on the photograph shared by Shrinate, we traced it to an X post uploaded on March 3 by Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi. In the post, he claimed the graves were being dug for “more than 160 innocent young girls who were killed in the US-Israeli bombing” of a primary school in Minab.
These are graves being dug for more than 160 innocent young girls who were killed in the US-Israeli bombing of a primary school. Their bodies were torn to shreds.
This is how “rescue” promised by Mr. Trump looks in reality.
From Gaza to Minab, innocents murdered in cold blood. pic.twitter.com/cRdJ3BELOn
— Seyed Abbas Araghchi (@araghchi) March 2, 2026
The photo was also used in reports published by NDTV, Hindustan Times, India Today, Deccan Herald and others.
We also found that Reuters had shared the photo on its official website on March 2, 2026, giving credit to the Iranian Foreign Media Department/West Asian News Agency. The caption said, “Graves are being prepared for the victims following a reported strike on a school in Minab, Iran, March 2, 2026.”
Probing further, we found that the official X handle of the embassy of the Islamic Republic of Iran in India had shared four photos of the burial site in Minab.
Images of the burial ceremony of martyred students in #Minab, Iran, following the attacks carried out by the criminal regimes of America and Israel. pic.twitter.com/fggmyLoPqp
— Iran in India (@Iran_in_India) March 3, 2026
We could geolocate the place and independently establish that it showed the Minab Cemetery in Iran. Below is a comparison between an image of the burial site from Google Maps and one from an X post by the official handle of the Iranian embassy.
Note that the photo that Rajdeep Sardesai shared was among the four posted by the embassy.
To sum up, the photos shared by Congress spokesperson Supriya Shrinate and journalist Rajdeep Sardesai as showing the mass burial of school children in Iran are authentic. The ‘fact-checks’ by pro-BJP handle Shashank Shekhar Jha are baseless and false.
Alt News has fact-checked false and misleading claims by Shashank Shekhar Jha several times in the past. Readers can access these stories here.
claim_text: Photo of mass-grave being dug apparently from COVID-hit Indonesia, and not Iran
