Authorities have launched a detailed investigation into the matter. ||| #फरीदाबाद फरीदाबाद से फिर बरामद हुआ विस्फोटक फरीदाबाद के सेक्टर 56 से विस्फोटक बरामद 50 से 60 किलो विस्फोटक बरामद क्राइम ब्रांच ने दो लोगों को गिरफ्तार किया @police_haryana #DelhiCarBlast #RajasthanWithNews18 pic.twitter.com/bnWdqEdec5 ||| मीडिया में एक भ्रामक खबर प्रचारित हो रही है कि से० 56 फरी० क्षेत्र में विस्फोटक सामग्री मिली है यह सामग्री शादी विवाह में चलने वाले पटाखे व उसका रॉ मैटेरियल है, इसका आतंकवादी गतिविधियों से कोई संबंध नहीं है। फरीदाबाद पुलिस इसका खंडन करती है, भ्रामक प्रचार ना करें @police_haryana

title: AI video of COAS Upendra Dwivedi saying Indian Army to reduce non-Hindu soldiers by 50% goes viral
body_text:
A video of the Chief of Army Staff, General Upendra Dwivedi, making controversial remarks about the Indian Army and claiming that by 2028, the number of non-Hindu soldiers would be cut by half is viral on social media. Users have circulated the video, alleging that this is an attempt to purge the Indian Army of those who don’t fit into the traditional Hindu caste hierarchy.
The full speech, as can be heard in the viral clip, reads: “Recently, our ranks have been debated whether non-Hindu castes should remain in the Indian Army. I’ve brought this concern to the Defence Minister, Rajnath Singh, and his response was unambiguous. By 2028, non-Hindu soldiers must be reduced by over 50%. We have begun selecting out those who refuse to embrace Hinduism. If anyone wishes to convert, they may receive a five-year extension. But we will judge their sincerity before granting promotions. Indian Army, we are told, is for Hindus – not for outsiders, or descendants of so-called invaders. This policy is not about merit or loyalty, it’s about purging diversity. As soldiers, we are being told to trade our oath for an ideology. History will remember what is being done in these halls.
X user @Baba_Thoka posted the video on November 4, writing: “Non-caste Hindus in the Army to be shown the door by 2028 because apparently, patriotism now comes with a gotra certificate.” (Archive)
Breaking News:
Hindutva in India finally takes its “One Nation, One Caste” dream to the barracks!
Non-caste Hindus in the Army to be shown the door by 2028 because apparently, patriotism now comes with a gotra certificate. pic.twitter.com/ebCdpPovZk— Baba Thoka (@Baba_Thoka) November 4, 2025
X user @InsiderWB also shared the video but later deleted it. (Archive)
Readers should note that this user has been called out by Alt News for amplifying misinformation several times in the past.
Several other users, such as @MehboobQadir4, @SadiasOfficial, @zaryabali720 and others also circulated the viral video. (Archives: 1, 2, 3)
The video, with the accompanying claim, was also viral on WhatsApp. Alt News received several requests on our helpline ( to verify the clip.
Fact Check
Recently, several manipulated videos of defence officers, including Lt Gen Rajiv Ghai and Colonel Sofia Qureshi, making controversial claims regarding the Indian Army had gone viral too; Alt News had debunked them at the time. However, that made us doubt the authenticity of the video featuring COAS General Upendra Dwivedi as well.
We conducted several relevant keyword searches to see if the speech had been reported on by any reliable news outlet, but found none.
Thereafter, we broke the viral clip down into keyframes. A reverse image search on one such keyframe led us to this video, uploaded by ANI on November 1.
#WATCH | Rewa, Madhya Pradesh | Chief of the Army Staff, General Upendra Dwivedi says, “Coming to TRS College was a great privilege for me, as I’ve been around this building since 1973, but I never entered. It was a huge source of inspiration for me… I was deeply inspired to… pic.twitter.com/AsA9uCugeM
— ANI (@ANI) November 1, 2025
The clip shows General Upendra Dwivedi giving a press byte at the TRS College in Madhya Pradesh, where he speaks about the lessons learnt from Operation Sindoor and lauded qualities like confidence, courage, collaboration, change, and clarity.
We went through the whole video to check if General Dwivedi had made the controversial remarks, but found that no such thing was said.
We then checked the viral video again and found the lip movements slightly inconsistent with the audio in some parts. This indicated the possibility that the viral video too was a deepfake, like the ones of Col Qureshi and LG Ghai.
To be sure, we ran the viral video through HIVE’s AI detection tool, which showed a 91.5% possibility of the clip containing AI-generated content.
Based on these findings, we could conclude that the viral video, which apparently shows the Chief of the Army Staff, General Upendra Dwivedi, claiming that the Indian Army has decided to reduce non-Hindu soldiers enlisted in the Army by 50% by the year 2028 is AI-generated and not genuine.
claim_text: The Chief of the Army Staff, General Upendra Dwivedi has said that the Indian Army promises to reduce non-caste Hindus in the Army by 50% by the year 2028
original_post_text: Breaking News: Hindutva in India finally takes its “One Nation, One Caste” dream to the barracks! Non-caste Hindus in the Army to be shown the door by 2028 because apparently, patriotism now comes with a gotra certificate. pic.twitter.com/ebCdpPovZk ||| #WATCH | Rewa, Madhya Pradesh | Chief of the Army Staff, General Upendra Dwivedi says, “Coming to TRS College was a great privilege for me, as I’ve been around this building since 1973, but I never entered. It was a huge source of inspiration for me… I was deeply inspired to… pic.twitter.com/AsA9uCugeM

title: Delhi Blast: Did police claim CNG cylinder led to explosion? No, fabricated statement by top cop goes viral
body_text:
After 13 people were killed in a high-intensity blast near Delhi’s Red Fort on the evening of November 10, speculation about the plausible reasons behind the explosion flooded social media.
According to police, a slow-moving vehicle — a Hyundai i20 car — exploded near the Red Fort metro’s gate 1 around 6:52 pm. While there have been some reports linking the blast to a terror module, the police are still investigating. However, several social media users claimed that the blast was owing to a CNG cylinder explosion in the car and not a terror attack. Note that CNG vehicles are common in Delhi.
A post by X page @TeamSaath on November 10 said that it was a “tragic CNG cylinder explosion” and attributed the information to special commissioner of police (law & order) Ravindra Singh Yadav. “The high casualty figure is because the blast happened in a crowded pick-up zone right outside Red Fort Metro Gate 1 during evening rush and resultant fire spread to nearby vehicles. As confirmed by Special CP Ravindra Yadav (Law & Order),” the post read.
The post has now been deleted.
X account @azaadfactcheck claimed the news of a terror attack was “disinformation by an India-military–run X handle from the DPRO Lucknow office”. Sharing the image of a CNG cylinder, the post also said: “However, Azaad has obtained an image of the CNG cylinder that actually exploded, with Delhi Police standing next to it.”
At the time of writing this report, the post had over 38,000 views. (Archive)
Azaad Fact Check has identified the spread of disinformation by an India-military–run X handle from the DPRO Lucknow office, which falsely claimed that the blast in Delhi was a terrorist attack. However, Azaad has obtained an image of the CNG cylinder that actually exploded, with… https://t.co/uWFA42uQ50 pic.twitter.com/Gzcqrzhd2r
— Azaad Fact Check (@azaadfactcheck) November 10, 2025
News outlet Republic initially claimed that police sources had ‘confirmed’ that the blast was caused by a CNG cylinder.
BREAKING | Red Fort Explosion Updates
What We Know:-NIA and NSG teams arrive at spot
-Eyewitness shares account from exact moment of explosion
-Police sources confirm CNG cylinder caused the blast
-Delhi NCR put on high alertTune in for more LIVE updates:… pic.twitter.com/NYFmArU7Lw
— Republic (@republic) November 10, 2025
Fact Check
To verify this, we ran a keyword search to check if Special Commissioner of police (Law & Order) Ravindra Yadav actually issued such a statement. We did not find any news reports that quoted him saying so. No such statement — text, audio or video — was issued by the X handles of Yadav or the Delhi police either.
When Alt News reached out to Special CP (Law & Order) Ravindra Yadav’s office regarding the claims, they denied that Yadav made any such statement and called the claims fake.
Further, we also came across an X post from the Press Information Bureau’s fact-check handle (@PIBFactCheck) that Special CP (Law & Order) Ravindra Yadav did not issue any such statement.
Some social media accounts are claiming that Special CP (Law & Order) Ravindra Yadav has confirmed that the recent blast in #Delhi was caused by a CNG explosion.#PIBFactCheck
❌ This claim is fake. No official from the Delhi Police has made any such statement.
▶️ The incident… pic.twitter.com/zC0f0qJDtE
— PIB Fact Check (@PIBFactCheck) November 10, 2025
On November 11, the Ministry of Home Affairs handed over the investigation to the National Investigation Agency (NIA). The NIA’s involvement hints that the government considers the blast to be an act of terror. However, an official statement is still awaited. Meanwhile, claims that the police said the blast was caused by a vehicle’s CNG cylinder exploding are fabricated and false.
claim_text: Delhi blast because of CNG cylinder explosion

However, Azaad has obtained an image of the CNG cylinder that actually exploded, with… https://t.co/uWFA42uQ50 pic.twitter.com/Gzcqrzhd2r ||| BREAKING | Red Fort Explosion Updates What We Know: ||| Some social media accounts are claiming that Special CP (Law & Order) Ravindra Yadav has confirmed that the recent blast in #Delhi was caused by a CNG explosion. #PIBFactCheck

title: CCTV footage shows tiger attacking man in Maharashtra? No, viral clip is AI-generated
body_text:
CCTV footage of a tiger pouncing on a man and dragging him away is viral on social media and is being shared as an incident from Brahmapuri, in Maharashtra’s Chandrapur district.
According to the timestamp on the footage, the incident occurred on October 31, 2025, around 6:42 PM.
X user Himmu (@Himmu86407253) shared the video, claiming that the CCTV recording was from the Brahmapuri forest guest house. (Archive)
सीसीटीवी रिकॉर्डिंग at ब्राह्मपुरी फारेस्ट गेस्ट हाउस. (चंद्रपुर डिस्ट्रिक)👇👇👇 pic.twitter.com/d4SGS2Fu6N
— Himmu (@Himmu86407253) November 7, 2025
The post garnered around 850,000 views.
Another X user, Harsh Panday (@harshpanday2015), also shared the video and made similar claims. (Archive)
The post has now been deleted.
Several other users made similar claims; a few of them can be seen in the gallery below.
The video and the accompanying claim are also viral on WhatsApp. Alt News received requests to verify the clip.
Fact Check
A closer examination of the video revealed multiple inconsistencies, raising doubts over its authenticity.
First, we noticed that in several frames, the man and the tiger appear to blend unnaturally with the background. Visuals below:
The second inconsistency was that the tiger’s movement caused a cloud of dust, but the leaves scattered on the ground remained completely still.
Thirdly, we noticed that after the tiger attacked the man, the shadow cast by the fallen chair was facing the wrong way.
Further, we also found an X post by the district information office, Chandrapur, from on November 7, 2025, stating that the Brahmpuri tiger attack video was fake and had been generated with the help of artificial intelligence.
ब्रम्हपुरीत वाघाच्या हल्ल्याचा व्हिडीओ बनावट
➡️ब्रम्हपुरी वनविभागातील वनविश्रामगृह येथे एका इसमावर वाघाच्या हल्ल्याचा व्हिडीओ बनावट असून सदर व्हिडीओ कृत्रिम बुद्धिमत्तेच्या (Artificial Intelligence – AI) साहाय्याने तयार केला आहे.
More information⬇️https://t.co/xXQg9woqRk pic.twitter.com/IDnFg9Kilb— DISTRICT INFORMATION OFFICE, CHANDRAPUR (@InfoChandrapur) November 7, 2025
PIB Maharashtra also debunked the claim, clarifying that the viral video was not genuine CCTV footage, but AI-generated.
A video is being shared widely on social media claiming to be the CCTV footage from Brahmapuri, Chandrapur (Maharashtra) showing an incident inside a forest guest house.
❌The claim is #FAKE
✅The video is AI-generated and not an actual CCTV recording.
✅ No such incident has… pic.twitter.com/aCd0WSU8QJ
— PIB in Maharashtra 🇮🇳 (@PIBMumbai) November 7, 2025
To sum up, the viral CCTV footage showing a tiger pouncing on a man and dragging him away from the Brahmapuri forest guest house in Maharashtra does not depict an actual incident and is likely generated using artificial intelligence.
claim_text: a cctv video showing a tiger attacking a man and dragging him away from the Brahmapuri Forest Guest House in Maharashtra

title: ‘Vote chori’: Rahul Gandhi says 223 voters used same image, Amit Malviya hears 1 woman voted 223 times
body_text:
Soon after Congress leader Rahul Gandhi’s press conference alleging massive voter fraud in the 2024 Haryana assembly elections, BJP IT cell chief Amit Malviya dismissed these charges, calling them laughable.
Dubbing it an ‘H Bomb’, Gandhi highlighted several discrepancies in voter lists, one of them being the same image used by 223 voters across two polling booths. He also claimed that a Brazilian model’s photo was apparently used 22 times across ten booths.
Mocking Gandhi, Malviya pointed out that if a woman voted 223 times across two booths, it would mean that she cast a vote every three minutes during the 11-hour polling period, which seemed unlikely to go unnoticed by officials. He said the remark reflected Gandhi’s ignorance and desperation for headlines ahead of Bihar’s polls, adding that such claims were a deliberate attempt to undermine public trust in democratic institutions like the Election Commission and the Supreme Court.
Rahul Gandhi’s press conferences on so-called Vote Chori are downright laughable.
Here’s one gem — he claimed that a woman voted 223 times across two booths.
Let’s do some simple maths: polling runs from 7 AM to 6 PM, an 11-hour window.
So, according to Rahul Gandhi, this woman…— Amit Malviya (@amitmalviya) November 5, 2025
Fact Check: Did Gandhi say a woman voted 223 times across two booths?
The short answer is no, he did not.
At the November 5 press conference, Gandhi spoke about the same image appearing against names of several voters on the electoral rolls at two booths in Haryana. Gandhi cited ‘the worst example’ and showed that one woman’s image was used against 223 different voter IDs.
The details appear at the 17:57-minute mark of the press conference above. Below is a transcript of what he says:
“This is the list of two polling booths. She appears 223 times in two polling booths. She can vote any amount of times she wants. Election Commission has the data on how many times she voted. We have a sense of it. Election Commission needs to tell us how many times this lady, whose name we don’t know, whose age we don’t know, but we know she occurs 223 times in two booths. In the Lok Sabha elections, she was there 223 booths in one booth — and then they decided to split it two booths.. right? So this is the reason Election Commission destroys CCTV footage because multiple times, the same person has the opportunity to vote. She can vote as Seema; she can vote as anything she wants. And this is not one example; this is the worst example. There are thousands.. of examples like this in Haryana. 200 votes, 50 votes, 100 votes, 150 votes.. Anybody can go and vote. Why are they doing it? Because they want to create space and what is the proof they are doing it, they are destroying CCTV records. If we ask for CCTV records of this booth, we will know 100% what happened in this booth.”
At no point did Rahul Gandhi claim that a single woman voted 223 times. His point was that these names acted as placeholder, fictitious or proxy voters, and that’s why the Election Commission was withholding CCTV footage. Gandhi argues that such voters, who likely never physically voted, were still being counted.
This stance was made clearer through a post made by Gandhi on X after the press conference. Citing the core points of the presser, he said,” एक ही फोटो के साथ 223 वोट एक बूथ में – नाम हर बार नया” (Translation: 223 votes in one booth with the same photo – a new name every time). As one can see, he does not explicitly say that the same woman voted 223 times in one day, as was misconstrued by Malviya.
वोट चोरी से हरियाणा में कांग्रेस की सरकार चोरी की गई है – और इसका ब्लैक एंड व्हाइट सबूत आपके सामने हैं।
– ब्राज़ील की एक मॉडल हरियाणा की वोटर लिस्ट में 10 बूथों पर 22 वोट – अलग-अलग नाम से
– एक ही फोटो के साथ 223 वोट एक बूथ में – नाम हर बार नया
– एक ही घर में 501 वोटर दर्ज – वो… pic.twitter.com/mCfNLc62P2— Rahul Gandhi (@RahulGandhi) November 5, 2025
As it stands, it is not true that LoP Rahul Gandhi claimed that the same voter voted 223 times across two booths in Haryana during the assembly elections in 2024. He points out an anomaly, which he insinuates was done intentionally to commit voter fraud; BJP leader Amit Malviya’s remarks quoting Gandhi are misleading.
claim_text: Rahul Gandhi has claimed that the same voter voted 223 times across two booths in Haryana in 2024
original_post_text: Rahul Gandhi’s press conferences on so-called Vote Chori are downright laughable. ||| वोट चोरी से हरियाणा में कांग्रेस की सरकार चोरी की गई है – और इसका ब्लैक एंड व्हाइट सबूत आपके सामने हैं।

title: Col Sofia Qureshi’s video on ‘saffronisation’ and using Indian Army for gimmicks ahead of Bihar Elections is fake
body_text:
A video of Colonel Sofia Qureshi calling out “saffronisation” in the Indian Army and saying that her job is to defend the country and not be an ideological vessel for the BJP or the RSS has gone viral with social media users claiming she dropped a “political bomb” on live television.
In the video, she is heard criticising the tri-services ‘Trishul’ exercise – a drill scheduled to be held between November 3 and 10, which is meant to enhance joint operational procedures among the armed forces – as a political gimmick ahead of the Bihar elections.
In the viral video (footage below), the army officer is heard saying: “Today, I stand here, not as a political pawn, but as a soldier of the Indian Army. For two-and-a-half years, I have served my country with pride, until I realized we were being used as props to divide our own citizens. The saffronization of our ranks is a betrayal for every oath we took. Our job is to defend India, not the ideology of BJP or RSS. These so-called ‘Trishul Exercises’ before the Bihar elections are nothing but theatre, to fool the nation. Uniforms are being painted in ideology, not honour. This must stop now. If speaking the truth costs my uniform, so be it. I still stand with India, not with hate.”
X user @Baba_Thoka shared the viral video on November 3. At the time of this article being written, the post managed to rack up more than 5 lakh views and was shared more than 1,600 times. (Archive)
X users @InsiderWB, @ConflictWatchX, @Shadowfox_11, @NomanMalik2701 and @78Alph also shared the viral video. (Archives: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
Fact Check
A similar video of another armed force officer, Lt Gen Rajiv Ghai, recently went viral and was fact-checked by Alt News. Since that video was fake, we were doubtful of this one’s authenticity as well, owing to the similarities in the viral content.
However, to verify whether Colonel Sofia Qureshi had indeed made such comments at a recent event, we conducted relevant keyword searches to check if any reliable news outlet had reported on this, but found none.
We then broke down the viral video into keyframes. A reverse image search on one of them led us to this X post, uploaded by ANI, on October 31.
#WATCH | Delhi: During Young Leaders Forum, Colonel Sofiya Qureshi says, “…Responsibilities for defending a nation are not just the military’s game; civilians also play a role in addressing external and internal threats… Over the years, the Indian army has trained officers… pic.twitter.com/4ssL6wam7s
— ANI (@ANI) October 31, 2025
ANI also uploaded the full speech on its YouTube channel.
According to the description, Col Qureshi was addressing the Young India Forum, organised by Chanakya Defence Dialogue of the Indian Army on the theme “Veer Yuva: Empowering Youth for India’s National Security.” We went through the speech and could not find any mention of the BJP, RSS, or Bihar Elections anywhere — which made us suspect that this may have been a digitally altered clip. The eye movements of Col Qureshi made the video seem artificially generated.
Thereafter, we ran the viral video through HIVE’s AI detection tool, which showed a 99% possibility of the clip containing AI-generated content.
Based on these findings, we believe that the viral video of Colonel Sofia Qureshi bashing the ‘Trishul Exercises’ as a political gimmick and talking about the ‘saffronisation’ of the Indian Army is not genuine and was likely generated using AI technology.
Also Read | AI-generated video of Donald Trump ‘roasting’ Indian Air Force goes viral and Did India’s army chief ‘admit’ to losing 6 jets & 250 soldiers in Op Sindoor? No, viral video is digitally altered
claim_text: Colonel Sofia Qureshi has criticized the 'saffronization' of the Indian Army, calling herself a prop for Muslim support
original_post_text: #WATCH | Delhi: During Young Leaders Forum, Colonel Sofiya Qureshi says, “…Responsibilities for defending a nation are not just the military’s game; civilians also play a role in addressing external and internal threats… Over the years, the Indian army has trained officers… pic.twitter.com/4ssL6wam7s

title: Did Rahul Gandhi say ‘I don’t get any disease, I have direct connection with God’? No, viral video is clipped
body_text:
A 15-second-long clip in which Rahul Gandhi says, “I don’t get any disease, I have a direct connection from above, I directly speak to God,” is being shared by social media users to mock the Congress leader for his bizarre statement.
X user Megh Updates @MeghUpdates shared the clip on October 30 and at the time of writing this report, the post had over 100,000 views and was reposted over 800 times. (Archive)
Readers should note that this user has been found spreading misinformation several times in the past.
“मुझे कोई बीमारी नहीं होती, मेरा डायरेक्ट ऊपर से कनैक्शन है…!! “
“I don’t get any disease, I have a direct connection from above…!! pic.twitter.com/nPM9Fdh4JA
— Megh Updates 🚨™ (@MeghUpdates) October 29, 2025
Another X user, राहुल देव Rahul Dev (@rahuldev2), also posted the same clip. This post received more than 124,000 views and was reposted over 200 times. (Archive)
This user has been fact-checked by Alt News before.
“मुझे कोई बीमारी नहीं होती, मेरा डायरेक्ट ऊपर से कनैक्शन है…!! ” pic.twitter.com/BQlsCY0ClE
— राहुल देव Rahul Dev (@rahuldev2) October 29, 2025
Several other users posted the same clip mocking the Congress leader for making a bizarre statement. Below are a few instances.
Fact Check
To verify the authenticity of this video, we broke it down into several keyframes and ran a reverse image search on some of them. This led us to an X post by news agency ANI on October 29, where a longer video of Gandhi’s speech from the same event was shared. In the caption, ANI mentioned that the event took place in Darbhanga, Bihar — where assembly elections are slated to take place this week.
In this 3:02-minute-long video, Gandhi can be heard talking about Prime Minister Narendra Modi taking a bath in the Yamuna River, and he goes on to say that the Prime Minister would go to any extent for the sake of votes. At the 1:09-minute mark, Gandhi says, “…No one can get into that water, it is so dirty. If you go into it, then you will fall sick and get infected right there. But Modiji did drama there… he created a little pond, you saw it, right? This is the state of India. They will do anything during elections. ‘Look, I have a 56-inch chest and I am taking a bath in the Yamuna. I don’t get any diseases; I have a direct connection with the above. I directly speak to God. Nothing will happen to me due to Yamuna’s water’. Then, they will install a pipe from behind and pour clean water inside. Then the media will show that Modiji took a bath in the Yamuna.”
#WATCH | Darbhanga, Bihar | Lok Sabha LoP Rahul Gandhi says, “PM Modi said that he will bathe on the Yamuna for Chhath. I said in the last meeting that we can get anything done from PM Modi during the elections. In the next meeting, if 200 people from the crowd ask PM Modi to… pic.twitter.com/x47nPB9gyp
— ANI (@ANI) October 29, 2025
From the above context, it is clear that Gandhi was mocking Modi when he said, “I don’t get any diseases; I have a direct connection with the above. I directly speak to God,” while talking about the prime minister taking a bath in the Yamuna.
It should be noted that ANI, in its caption, translated the clip to English except the part where Gandhi mockingly said that PM Modi has a ‘direct connection with God’.
Interestingly, on October 25, The Hindu reported that according to the Delhi Pollution Control Committee’s (DPCC) monthly report, the Yamuna River’s water quality in Delhi worsened and was unfit for bathing. However, right before Chhath Puja, Delhi Water Minister Parvesh Sahib Singh stated that there had been a 90% reduction in faecal coliform levels in the Yamuna from 2024.
However, according to a Newslaundry investigation, instead of reducing the pollution in the Yamuna, the Delhi government had been adding clean water to the river.
claim_text: Rahul Gandhi said he has direct connection with God, never gets diseases

I said in the last meeting that we can get anything done from PM Modi during the elections. In the next meeting, if 200 people from the crowd ask PM Modi to… pic.twitter.com/x47nPB9gyp

title: Photos from Bangladeshi films shared to back cooked-up ‘Love Jihad’ story
body_text:
An image of a woman with what looks like burn injuries on her face is viral on social media alongside the photo of a couple. It is being claimed that the Hindu woman was attacked by her Muslim partner and that their relationship was a case of ‘Love Jihad’ — a conspiracy theory peddled by Hindutva groups which claims that Muslim men trap Hindu women into romantic/spousal relationships with the motive to convert them into Islam.
X user Bharatrastrasena (@Bharatrastrsena) shared the above-mentioned images on October 29. The photo of the couple has the man labelled as ‘Muslim’ and the woman labelled as ‘Hindu’. In the caption, the user claims that photo of the couple is from 2023 and that of the injured face from 2025. It further says, “…This Honda Sherni married Abdul Adil Khan in 2023, changed her name from Nandini Mandal to Zara Islam, and converted to Islam. Many Hindus tried to reason with her, but she wouldn’t listen. This Honda lioness was completely engrossed in Abdul’s love…” (Archive)
At the time of this report being written, the post has received over 640,000 views, close to 10,000 likes and has been reposted over 4,000 times.
Another Honda Sherni ruined in Abdul’s love, read it completely and understand it. 🤡😣
This Honda Sherni married Abdul Adil Khan in 2023, changed her name from Nandini Mandal to Zara Islam, and converted to Islam. Many Hindus tried to reason with her, but she wouldn’t listen.… pic.twitter.com/DubZcH2X7A
— Bharatrastrasena (@Bharatrastrsena) October 29, 2025
Another X user, @Hindulegalsena, also posted the viral image. The caption is in Hindi. It can be translated to, “Even her Abdul turned out to be the same. Many people had warned her to stay away from Abdul, that he’d rob her, put her in the freezer, but she didn’t listen; she was already drowned in Abdul’s love for 2 years, Abdul satisfied his lust with her and then kicked her out of his house. Now she’s crying, asking people for help”.
Several other users posted the same set of images, claiming that the woman, who was a Hindu, was attacked by her partner, who was a Muslim named Abdul Adil Khan. Below are a few instances:
The phrase ‘Honda Sherni’ is a commonly used abusive term in the vocabulary of Hindutva flagbearers. A possible distortion of ‘Hindu Sherni’, it is generally used to describe women who publicly acknowledge being in interfaith relationships.
We also noticed that some other users made a different claim about the ‘injured’ woman. X user @Marssxyz shared a video of her in which she is seen crying. The video ends with footage of a woman lighting a firecracker, the fire from which splashes on her face. Though there is no way to ascertain from the video whether they are the same person, the user claims that the burn injuries on her face were caused by an accident during Diwali.
The girl was burnt during Diwali.
Imagine these RW are spreading her image saying Muslim attacked. They don’t even care about the girl. It’s just the hate ! Hope their mothers and sisters see this pic.twitter.com/mZvIMFotAN
— Mars (@Marssxyz) October 29, 2025
Fact Check
To verify the claims and find out more about the woman, we broke down the video posted by @Marssxyz into multiple keyframes and ran a reverse image search on a few of them. This led us to a Facebook page by the name Jouty Islam where the same video had been posted. According to the bio, the page belongs to Bangladeshi actress Jouty Islam.
The caption of the post in Bengali can be translated as, “There’s an unknown story behind everything in life — a story that no one wants to know or understand”. The post also carried the following hashtags: “#foryoupagereels #joutyislam #BTS #BanglaNatok (Bengali Drama) #shooting”. The findings indicated that the video might be behind-the-scenes footage of a Bengali drama.
💔💔
Posted by Jouty Islam on Saturday 25 October 2025
In the comments, the uploader has clarified that the bruises on the face is ‘part of a shooting’.
We found that the page also shared a poster for a drama on October 30, where the same woman, who is seen in the viral image and the above video, can be seen with bruises on her face. The poster is for a fictional piece named “Ami Nusrat Bolchi” (I am Nusrat). The cast has the following names — Yamin Shimul, Jouty Islam and Tinu Karim.
“আমি নুসরাত বলছি“
Coming soon……….Posted by Jouty Islam on Thursday 30 October 2025
Actor Yamin Shimul also shared the video of the ‘bruised’ woman crying and clearly mentioned that it was a teaser for an upcoming movie.
As far as the image of the couple is concerned, we found a very similar photo on the same actress’s timeline. It is a poster for another Bangladeshi film, called “Bidhoba Bou” (Widow wife). This was shared in February.
Alhamdulillah 🙏
Choice View MediaPosted by Jouty Islam on Tuesday 18 February 2025
See the comparison below:
Therefore, from the above findings, it is clear that the set of viral images of a couple and a woman with burn injuries on her face are not from any real incident or a case of “Love Jihad”. Both are related to Bangladeshi movies. One is a poster and the other is a behind-the-scene photo of actress Jouty Islam with makeup on.
claim_text: Hindu woman's face burnt by her Muslim partner
original_post_text: Another Honda Sherni ruined in Abdul’s love, read it completely and understand it. 🤡😣 ||| The girl was burnt during Diwali.

title: Hindu youths desecrated temples in UP with ‘I love Muhammad’ graffiti to frame Muslim peers, arrested
body_text:
Visuals with the words “I love Muhmad” and “I love Mamud” spray-painted on the walls of two temples are viral on social media. In one of the videos showing the graffiti, the person recording the video is heard saying that the visuals are from a Shiv temple in the Bulak Garhi village in Uttar Pradesh.
Pro-Right social media user @KreatelyMedia, whose posts have often been fact-checked by Alt News, shared the visuals, blaming ‘Islamists’ for desecrating the temple. “As the population grows, so will their terror,” the user remarked. (Archive)
🚨Islamists wrote “I love Mohammad” on the walls of a Shiv Ji’s temple in Aligarh, UP
जैसे जैसे आबादी बढ़ेगी, वैसे वैसे इनका आतंक बढ़ेगा pic.twitter.com/lY1wJkhiFJ
— Kreately.in (@KreatelyMedia) October 25, 2025
Another user, @SonOfBharat7, also shared the video, blaming ‘Jihadis’ for the incident and claimed that this was done to incite riots. The post garnered nearly 53,000 views and nearly 2,000 reshares at the time this article was written. (Archive)
यूंही दुनिया नहीं थूकती.. इन थूकलमानो पर
शिव मंदिर पर लिख दिया I Love Muhammad
जी हाँ… अलीगढ़ के शिव मंदिर पर जिहादियों
ने आई लव मुहम्मद लिखकर दंगा भड़काने का
प्रयास किया हैकोई इन जिहादियों को बता दो.. फिर हम खुदाई
करवा देंगे, तो मस्जिद से शिव जी के नाग ही निकलेंगे 🖐️ pic.twitter.com/UZTAr3MetQ— Deepak Sharma (@SonOfBharat7) October 25, 2025
Several others also shared the video, claiming ‘Islamist goons’ and ‘Jihadis’ were to blame. (Archives 1, 2, 3, 4)
Fact Check
To verify the claims and understand what happened, we looked for a statement by the Aligarh police. Aligarh police’s senior superintendent, in his initial statement, revealed that four temples had been desecrated in two neighbouring villages — Bulak Garhi and Bhagwanpur. He also stated that they were thoroughly investigating all aspects, including a previous dispute that had come to notice.
थाना लोधा- देवस्थलों पर धार्मिक नारा लिखे जाने की सूचना पर वरिष्ठ पुलिस अधीक्षक अलीगढ़ द्वारा ग्राम बुलकगढ़ी व ग्राम भगवानपुर के सभी देवस्थलों का निरीक्षण किया गया, 4 देवस्थलों पर लिखे धार्मिक स्लोगन को हटवा दिया गया है, गाँव वालों से भी वार्ता की गई है, गाँव में पूर्व का एक… pic.twitter.com/OT5cp0epxU
— ALIGARH POLICE (@aligarhpolice) October 25, 2025
One of the images shared by the Aligarh police shows a police officer interacting with two individuals in front of a pink temple. According to a Dainik Bhaskar report, slightly different graffiti was also sprayed on a white temple.
In a later press conference SSP Neeraj Jadon revealed that four Hindu youth, Jishant Kumar from Bulak Garhi, and Abhishek, Akash and Dilip from Bhagwapur, were arrested in connection with the case. He also said that one man named Rahul was absconding. The police retrieved CCTV footage, call detail records to catch the culprits and noted that text on the walls had spelling mistakes. The police also went through older cases and found that one of the main accused, Jishant Singh, fought with a man named Mustakeem, while Rahul had a tiff with Gul Mohammed. In order to frame their adversaries, the accused made the graffiti on the temple walls. “They thought that by making such religious graffiti, the police would promptly investigate and arrest their opposing parties…” said SSP Jadon.
यूपी –
अलीगढ़ में मंदिर की दीवार पर “आई लव मोहम्मद” लिखने वाले 4 आरोपी जीशांत सिंह, आकाश सारस्वत, दिलीप शर्मा, अभिषेक सारस्वत गिरफ्तार !!जीशान्त सिंह का मुस्तकीम से झगड़ा हुआ। दोनों ने एक–दूसरे पर FIR कराई। पुलिस दोनों केस में चार्जशीट लगा चुकी है। मुस्तकीम को नए केस में… https://t.co/D8HeGTpBlT pic.twitter.com/NtxgYRUvY0
— Sachin Gupta (@SachinGuptaUP) October 30, 2025
Thus, it was actually some Hindu youths who sprayed walls of Hindu temples in two villages of UP’s Aligarh with different versions of the ‘I love Muhammed’ graffiti. Claims by social media users that this was the work of ‘Islamist Jihadis’ is false and baseless. According to the police, the miscreants desecrated the temple to frame their Muslim adversaries in a fit of revenge owing to personal tiffs.
claim_text: 'I love Muhmad' spray-painted on temple walls by Muslims

title: Rahul Gandhi did not call Chhath puja a ‘drama’; claim by Modi, BJP camp false
body_text:
Rahul Gandhi’s comments on Chhath puja at an election rally in Bihar have raised a political storm. It is being claimed that the Congress MP called Chhath puja, celebrated primarily in north Indian states, a ‘drama’.
Prime Minister Narendra Modi, while addressing a rally in Bihar’s Muzaffarpur on October 30, called Congress and RJD “shameless” while criticizing Gandhi’s comments. Slamming the Opposition coalition in Bihar, Modi claimed, “Congress and RJD have insulted Chhathi Maiyya for votes” by calling Chhath puja a “drama”. “For them (RJD-Congress), the puja of Chhathi maiya is a drama,” these were the PM’s exact words. (5.34 minute onwards in the following video.)
#BiharElections2025 || PM Modi addresses a mega rally in Bihar’s Muzaffarpur.
“Congress & RJD have insulted Chhathi Maiya for votes… Chhath Puja is a mere drama for the Oppn”- PM @NarendraModi slams Mahagathbandhan#Nov14WithTimesNow pic.twitter.com/06OZOCDyQ4
— TIMES NOW (@TimesNow) October 30, 2025
The deputy chief minister of Bihar, Samrat Choudhary, shared a clip of Rahul Gandhi’s speech and remarked that Gandhi had no regard for Bihar’s culture and faith, alleging that Gandhi attributed Chhath Mahaparv as “drama”. (Archive)
छठ महापर्व को ड्रामा कहकर राहुल गांधी ने फिर दिखा दिया—
उन्हें बिहार की आस्था और संस्कृति से कोई सरोकार नहीं।बिहार याद रखेगा ये अपमान! pic.twitter.com/WMChhVVguI
— Samrat Choudhary (@samrat4bjp) October 29, 2025
BJP supporter Raushan Sinha, who peddles misinformation and communal propaganda on a regular basis from his X handle, @MrSinha_, claimed that Rahul Gandhi had said “Chhath puja is a drama”. He added that Gandhi had begun the Bihar election campaign by “abusing Hindus”. (Archive)
Chhath puja is a drama – Rahul Gandhi
He started his Bihar election campaign with abusing Hindus… pic.twitter.com/PDEeFdMyV6
— Mr Sinha (@MrSinha_) October 29, 2025
The same claim was also made by X user Political Kida (@PoliticalKida), who calls themselves “Lie Detector, Hypocrisy Slayer.” They wrote, “Rahul Gandhi just called Chhath Puja a drama”. (Archive)
Fact Check
We reviewed Rahul Gandhi’s speech delivered on October 29, 2025, at Muzzafarpur and found that he did not use the word ‘drama’ for the deity Chhath or the rituals associated with her puja. Instead, he used the term ‘drama’ while criticizing PM Modi’s performance of it.
In the part of his pre-poll address that has gone viral, the Congress leader’s exact words were: “Ek taraf Yamuna, jisme gannda pani, itna gannda ki koi piley, ya utar jaye toh bimar paad jaye. Aur ek taraf chotasa Modi ji ke liye talab. Dur dur se pipe se paani ata hai. Ek Hindustan, Modi ji wala Hindustan.
Agar Modi ji ko drama karna hai… Chatth Puja ka drama karna hai, toh pani aa jayega, video camera aa jayenge. Saaf pani, aur usike 10 gaaj dur Hindustan ki sacchai.
Ek taraf natak, Ambani Adani ka Hindustan, Narendra Modi ka drama, aur dusri taraf Hindustan ki sacchai, gannda pani aur bimari… TV pe wo pipe dikkh gaya, ki Narendra Modi ji nahane ayenge, Baki Hindustan gannde pani meh nahayega. Aur Narendra Modi ji k liye saaf pani ayega pipe meh, talab banaya jayega, aur Modi ji drama karenge. Yeh pure Hindustan neh dekha.”
Translation: “On one side, there’s the Yamuna — its water so filthy that if someone drinks it or even steps into it, they’ll fall ill. And on the other side, there’s a small pond made especially for Modi ji. Clean water is brought from far away through pipes. Thre is India and there is Modi ji’s India.
If Modi ji wants to put on a drama… if he wants to do a Chhath Puja drama, then suddenly the water appears, the cameras appear. Clean water for Modi ji — and just ten yards away, the real India, with dirty water and disease.
On one side, there’s the performance — the India of Ambani, Adani, and Narendra Modi’s drama. And on the other side, there’s the truth of India — dirty water and suffering. The whole country saw it on TV — that pipe through which clean water was being brought for Narendra Modi to bathe, while the rest of India bathes in polluted water. For Modi ji, a pond is built, clean water flows in through a pipe, and then Modi ji performs his drama. The entire country witnessed this.”
It is clear from a mere perusal of these lines that the word ‘drama’ referred to what Gandhi perceived as PM Modi’s performance of Chhatth Puja. Gandhi again mentioned the word, drama, later in his speech, but surely did not refer to Chhath Puja.
At 59:18 seconds, Gandhi again said, “Drama bana rakha hai.. Ek taraf Modiji drama kar rahe hai, kehte hai, ‘yeh dekho bhaiyya meh Yamuna ji meh nahaya hu.’ Waha koi Yamuna ji nehi hai. Waha ye talaab hai. Narendra Modi ji apne swimming pool meh nahane gaye, apne talaab meh nahane gaye. Unko Yamuna se kuch lena dena nehi, unko Chhath puja se kuch lena dena nehi. Unhe sirf aapka vote chahiye.”
Translation: “It’s a drama… On one side, Modi ji performs a drama saying, ‘Look, I’m bathing in the Yamuna.’ But in reality, there was no Yamuna there — it was a pond. Narendra Modi went to bathe in his own swimming pool, the pond. He has got nothing to do with the Yamuna or Chhath Puja. All he wants is your vote…”
The controversy arose with the reports that the Prime Minister was to take a dip at Delhi’s Vasudev Ghat on October 28, to offer Chhath prayers. AAP leader Saurabh Bharadwaj did a video from the spot and accused the BJP of creating a “fake” Yamuna by filling an artificial pool for Modi. In the end, however, he did not visit the site.
To sum up, the claims by PM Modi and the BJP camp that Rahul Gandhi had called Chhath Puja a ‘drama’ is false.
claim_text: Rahul Gandhi called Chhath puja a mere drama
original_post_text: #BiharElections2025 || PM Modi addresses a mega rally in Bihar’s Muzaffarpur. ||| छठ महापर्व को ड्रामा कहकर राहुल गांधी ने फिर दिखा दिया— उन्हें बिहार की आस्था और संस्कृति से कोई सरोकार नहीं। ||| Chhath puja is a drama – Rahul Gandhi

title: Video of drunk man petting, offering alcohol to Pench tiger is AI-generated
body_text:
A 6-second clip, ostensibly CCTV footage, showing a man petting a tiger in the middle of an empty street and offering the animal some liquor he was holding, is being widely shared as an incident from Madhya Pradesh, where a drunk labourer mistook the tiger for a giant cat.
According to those sharing the video — the man, 52-year-old Raju Patel — was drunk and on his way back home after playing cards and allegedly mistook a tiger, which had wandered off from the nearby Pench Tiger Reserve, as a big cat. Users also claimed that the tiger, which was roaming the village for hours, created panic among residents. According to several users, the tiger was finally brought under control by forest officials, who drove the animal back into the woods with the help of tranquillizers. The fiasco turned Patel into a local legend, as the news of the bizarre encounter spread.
X user @808constituent posted the viral clip on October 23, 2025. At the time this article was written, the post had garnered more than 60,000 views. (Archive)
On October 4, 2025, in Pench, India, a surreal moment was captured on CCTV. The photo shows Raju Patel, a 52-year-old laborer, patting a tiger he mistook for a “big cat” after a late-night card game. Tipsy from homemade liquor, Raju stumbled onto a street where a sub-adult Bengal… pic.twitter.com/FXbZsGeawy
— Constituent 🇺🇸🌺🐦🕊️ 🕉️ (@808constituent) October 23, 2025
Greater Andhra News, a news portal covering Andhra Pradesh and Telangana, also shared the viral video on October 29, calling it a “bizzare encounter” shared through WhatsApp . (Archive)
Several other users, such as @kiranpatel1977, @RussianBerner, and others also circulated the claim. (Archives 1, 2)
An Instagram user named Riddhi Ranthambore Safari also shared the clip.
View this post on Instagram
Alt News received requests on its WhatsApp helpline to verify the authenticity of the video.
Fact Check
To verify the authenticity of the claims, we first checked if the event was corroborated by any reputed news outlet or local forest authorities, but could find none.
The official X handle of Pench Tiger Reserve did not post about the alleged incident either.
On closer inspection, we noticed several visual discrepancies in the viral clip, which indicate that the video may have been artificially generated. For instance, as the man pets the tiger, the neck of the liquor bottle diminishes in size and is then restored to its original size. Moreover, in the background, the movement of two people, sitting on the porch of the house, appears distorted, another characteristic feature of such artificially generated clips. Screenshots below:
Alt News tried contacting the Pench Tiger Reserve, but our calls went unanswered. A report published by The Sootr, on October 29, quoting the deputy director of the reserve, Rajneesh Singh, dismissed the video and said it was not from Pench. He also pointed out the impossibility of such an encounter between a drunk man and a wild animal.
Based on this, Alt News was able to conclude that the viral clip is likely AI-generated. Although sightings of tigers are not entirely rare in the vicinity of the tiger reserve, no official statement records a tiger going missing on October 4 from Pench. The bizarre encounter between the tiger and the drunk man, it would seem, was artificially created and not a real event. Several such CCTV footage-like videos made using AI showing animals have been viral on social media lately.
claim_text: Tiger from Pench Tiger Reserve encountered intoxicated man on street, left unharmed

The photo shows Raju Patel, a 52-year-old laborer, patting a tiger he mistook for a “big cat” after a late-night card game. Tipsy from homemade liquor, Raju stumbled onto a street where a sub-adult Bengal… pic.twitter.com/FXbZsGeawy |||

title: Viral video of News18’s Amish Devgan touching Amit Shah’s shoe is fake
body_text:
A clip showing News18 managing editor Amish Devgan sitting with Home Minister Amit Shah and holding his shoe with both hands to help him pick up his leg is being widely shared on social media with snide remarks about the state of journalism in India.
X user Dr Aniruddha Malpani, MD (@malpani) shared the above-mentioned clip on October 26, sarcastically remarking that Devgan was the “role model of Indian journalism”. The post had over a million views and was reposted over 1,000 times at the time of writing this. (Archive)
The role model of Indian journalism ! pic.twitter.com/51daIsNutH
— Dr Aniruddha Malpani, MD (@malpani) October 26, 2025
An Instagram page, @thepolity_hub, also shared the same video with a caption in Hindi that translates to, “Amit Shah has made Amish Devgan sit on the tip of his shoe”.
View this post on Instagram
Several other users shared the same video, mocking the state of Indian media. Below are a few instances.
Fact Check
To verify the authenticity of this video, we broke down the clip into several keyframes and ran a reverse image search on a few of them. This led us to a YouTube video from May 23, 2024 on the News18 MP Chhattisgarh channel, where Devgan interviewed Amit Shah. At the time, Shah was campaigning for the 2024 Lok Sabha elections.
During the course of the interview, Devgan spent the day with Shah, following his poll campaigns in Odisha and West Bengal, and travelled with him in the same helicopter. However, at no point did the scene that appears in the viral clip appear in the full-length video.
We also found a post on Amish Devgan’s Instagram page from August 25, 2024, where he shared an image with Shah inside the helicopter. This image is the same one that appears in the first keyframe of the viral clip.
View this post on Instagram
Below is a comparison.
Based on this and the fact that the original interview did not have any such thing, led us to think the viral clip may have been generated using AI by feeding the above image with prompts.
On October 28, Amish Devgan also clarified on X that the viral clip was AI-generated and that he would take legal action.
Important Update:
I want to address the recent circulation of fake AI-generated videos targeting me. I assure everyone that I take these malicious actions seriously.
I will be taking strict legal action against those responsible and filing a criminal complaint with appropriate…— Amish Devgan (@AMISHDEVGAN) October 28, 2025
Thus, the viral clip showing the News18 anchor holding Amit Shah’s shoe is not a genuine video and likely generated using artificial intelligence.
claim_text: News18 anchor Amish Devgan touches HM Amit Shah's shoe
original_post_text: The role model of Indian journalism ! pic.twitter.com/51daIsNutH ||| Important Update: I want to address the recent circulation of fake AI-generated videos targeting me. I assure everyone that I take these malicious actions seriously. I will be taking strict legal action against those responsible and filing a criminal complaint with appropriate…

title: Video from Paraguay falsely shared as Bangladeshi infiltrator robbing Hindu family’s house in India
body_text:
A CCTV video showing a man scaling a barbed wire-lined wall and stealing a bicycle is circulating on the internet, with the claim that he was an illegal Bangladeshi infiltrator who robbed a Hindu businessman’s house in India.
X user Baba Banaras (@RealBababanaras) shared the video. He identified the man as Mohammed Zubair and said he was an illegal Bangladeshi infiltrator who was later arrested for stealing from a Hindu businessman’s house in India. The user expressed concerns over the “growing threat to India’s internal security, from theft to terror”, due to the presence of illegal Rohingyas and Bangladeshis. (Archive)
Illegal Bangladeshi infiltrator Mohammad Zubair was caught on CCTV stealing from a Hindu businessman’s house. Police later arrested him. Such illegal Rohingya & Bangladeshi elements are a growing threat to India’s internal security, from theft to terror. Deport them all. pic.twitter.com/v2vzCSH2DD
— Baba Banaras™ (@RealBababanaras) October 23, 2025
The video garnered around 24000 views. Users should note that this X user, Baba Banaras, peddles communal disinformation and propaganda on a regular basis.
Another user, Deepaktrilokani, shared the video on Threads with similar claims.
Fact Check
A reverse image search on a few keyframes from the video led us a Facebook page named Paraguay News, which had uploaded the same video on May 1, 2025. The caption explicitly mentioned that the incident took place in Barrio Jara, Asunción, the Capital of Paraguay.
The caption, when translated to English, reads,” A thief bypassed security wires and stole a bicycle from a house. A security camera captured the moment the lone criminal scaled the fence of a house, which was topped with razor wire. He made off with a bike and fled the scene. It happened in Barrio Jara, Asunción.”
▪️Delincuente burló alambres de seguridad y hurtó una bicicleta de una vivienda😱
Una cámara de seguridad captó el momento en que el solitario malviviente escaló la reja de un domicilio que contaba con alambres de cuchillas afiladas. Este se alzó con una bici y huyó del lugar.
Ocurrió en Barrio Jara, Asunción.
Posted by Noticiero Paraguay on Thursday 1 May 2025
Further investigation led us to a repport by SNT Paraguay, a Paraguayan news outlet, which shared a video report on April 30, 2025, which included an identical video. The report stated that the incident took place in Asunción, Paraguay.
View this post on Instagram
We found several media outlets, like Radio Caritas UC and Canal_e_py, reporting the same incident.
To sum up, a video of a man scaling a fence-like wall and stealing a bicycle in Asunción, Paraguay, is being falsely shared as an illegal Bangladeshi infiltrator stealing from a Hindu businessman’s home in India.
claim_text: A CCTV video showing a illegal Bangladeshi infiltrator climbing a barbed wire wall and stealing a bicycle from a Hindu businessman in India
original_post_text: Illegal Bangladeshi infiltrator Mohammad Zubair was caught on CCTV stealing from a Hindu businessman’s house. Police later arrested him. Such illegal Rohingya & Bangladeshi elements are a growing threat to India’s internal security, from theft to terror. Deport them all. pic.twitter.com/v2vzCSH2DD

title: More dislikes than likes on ‘Modi hai to mumkin hai’ music video? Claims, counter-claims by Cong, BJP. Who’s right?
body_text:
In the run-up to the Bihar assembly elections, a song, “Modi hai to mumkin hai”, released on YouTube on September 22 by T Series, has sparked a debate. Several social media users, including leaders from opposition parties, noted that the video received a large number of dislikes. To support this, they shared screenshots showing both likes and dislikes, where the number of dislikes outnumbered likes.
The video features Bollywood stars, such as Rajkummar Rao, Varun Dhawan, Arshad Warsi, and Vikrant Massey.
Claiming that the song was unpopular, Congress spokesperson Supriya Shrinate shared a screenshot on October 16 and wrote, “The song ‘Modi Hai To Mumkin Hai’ has caused a lot of embarrassment, it has 16 lakh views, the number of likes is 13,000, while the number of dislikes is 1 lakh.”
‘मोदी है तो मुमकिन है’ गाने ने बड़ी फजीहत करवा दी भाई लोगों
👀Views: 16 लाख
👍Likes: 13,000
👎Dislikes: 1 लाखऔर कमेंट तो बस पूछिए ही मत! pic.twitter.com/PghxzYQFqy
— Supriya Shrinate (@SupriyaShrinate) October 16, 2025
Many users shared screenshots where the number of dislikes is much higher than likes.
Subsequently, BJP IT cell head Amit Malviya responded to these claims and said that Congress used “fake screenshots” to claim that the song had over 1 lakh dislikes. He cited the “fact” that YouTube stopped showing the number of dislikes in 2021.
In its desperation, the Congress has now resorted to using fake screenshots to claim that the popular song ‘Modi Hai To Mumkin’ released by T-Series has over 100K dislikes.
Fact: YouTube stopped displaying dislike counts back in 2021. Song has 1.7Mn views.pic.twitter.com/odd6aAUrkf
— Amit Malviya (@amitmalviya) October 16, 2025
Fact Check
Has YouTube Stopped Showing Dislike Count?
In early 2021, YouTube experimented with the number of dislikes on videos to protect creators from harassment and targeted dislikes, which were particularly used against lesser-known creators.
In this experiment, YouTube found that when the number of dislikes was not visible, users were less likely to engage in aggressive ‘disliking’. While the ‘dislike’ button was visible and could be used, since the number of dislikes was not displayed, it was less likely for the video to be targeted by raising the number of dislikes.
Therefore, YouTube decided to officially hide dislike counts from viewers starting in November 2021 to create an inclusive and respectful environment, enabling creators to feel safe expressing themselves.
However, if creators want to see how a video is performing, they can see the exact number of dislikes on YouTube Studio. Viewers, too, can still dislike videos to make their recommendations more private and refined. The only change is that the number of dislikes is no longer visible on the video itself. Additionally, YouTube also removed access to public dislike data from developers using the YouTube Application Programming Interface (API) since December 13, 2021.
So, Are Screenshots of the Dislike Count Fake?
The answer is not as simple. Users who shared screenshots of dislike counts likely use a browser extension called Return YouTube Dislike (RYD), or third-party applications that use this API, or websites that display dislike data using this API. For example, in the video below, a browser extension displays the number of dislikes on a YouTube video. The difference before and after using the browser extension is clear in the video.
How does the Return YouTube Dislike (RYD) Tool Find the Number of Dislikes?
Interestingly, the number of dislikes displayed by this tool is not a real-time count, but estimated data derived based on the likes and dislikes of all users who use the Return YouTube Dislike (RYD) extension or third-party application. Based on this sample and the ‘likes’ on a video by all users, the tool arrives at an estimated value on the number of dislikes a video has.
How is This Estimated Data Calculated?
The estimated data of ‘dislikes’ is based on three kinds of specific and accurate data:
1. How many of those who use the RYD extension have ‘liked’ the video
2. How many of those who use the RYD extension have disliked or hit the thumbs down icon
3. The total number of likes the video has (not just those who have the RYD extension)
Based on these three data points, it arrives at an estimated dislike count. According to its FAQs on GitHub, here’s how that is done:
By going to the RYD API, data for the video ‘Modi Hai To Mumkin Hai’ can be found. Here, the number of likes by RYD extension users is 1,378 and dislikes by RYD extension users is 28,706. The total number of likes on the video is 51,001. Based on this, the number of estimated dislikes on this video stands at 4,37,286. To reiterate, this is estimated data calculated using a formula displayed on all platforms (Android, iOS, web browser extensions) using the Return YouTube Dislike (RYD) API and by other dislike counter online tools. However, such data can take a few minutes to a few hours to update.
So, Amit Malviya’s claim that people are sharing fake screenshots of the number of ‘dislikes’ on the ‘Modi Hai To Mumkin Hai’ video is not true. The screenshots are not fake. That said, however, his claim that YouTube stopped showing the dislike count is true. While YouTube has stopped showing the number of dislikes, several APIs and tools allow such estimations to be made, and the viral screenshots are based on calculations by one of these tools.
However, readers should note that data in the screenshots regarding the Modi song cannot be relied on completely to make assertions on its unpopularity because these are derived and not organic or provided by YouTube. It is estimated from a sample of those using a browser extension that tracks such dislikes and likes, making it susceptible to manipulation.
claim_text: More dislikes than likes on Modi hai to Mumkin hai video; YouTube Dislike screenshots are fake
original_post_text: ‘मोदी है तो मुमकिन है’ गाने ने बड़ी फजीहत करवा दी भाई लोगों ||| In its desperation, the Congress has now resorted to using fake screenshots to claim that the popular song ‘Modi Hai To Mumkin’ released by T-Series has over 100K dislikes.

title: Video of deputy COAS Lt Gen Rajiv Ghai talking about saffronisation of Indian Army is a deepfake
body_text:
A 35-second-long video showing the deputy chief of army staff, Lieutenant General Rajiv Ghai, purportedly criticising the growing politicisation and ‘saffronisation’ of the Indian Army and stating that it fuels internal distrust and erodes core values of the armed forces is viral on social media
In the viral clip, Ghai is seen giving a speech. He says, “As a senior officer who has devoted decades to the service of this uniform, I say this with deep concern. The growing influence of saffron politics is corroding the core values of the Indian Army. Our strength has always been rooted in discipline, unity, and constitutional duty – not in religious polarisation. When ideology starts dictating loyalty, it fractures morale, fuels internal distrust, and weakens combat effectiveness. If this trend continues, we will not be defeated by an external enemy, but by the political poison spreading within our own ranks.”
X user @Baba_Thoka posted the viral video on October 23, claiming that Ghai said they would resist attempts to make the army subservient to Hindutva. Since then, it has been viewed almost 40,000 times. (Archive)
🚨Breaking News:
Lt Gen Rajiv Ghai (Retd) slams #BJP & #NarendraModi’s politics, warns that any saffronization or politicization of the Indian Armed Forces will destroy morale.
“We will resist all attempts to make the Indian Army subservient to Hindutva.” pic.twitter.com/1HKnvh0x4V— Baba Thoka (@Baba_Thoka) October 23, 2025
Alt News also received multiple requests on its WhatsApp helpline (+91 76000 11160) to verify the authenticity of the video.
Fact Check
We noticed a Republic TV logo at the top-left corner of the video. Below the ‘R’ of the logo, there was also some garbled, distorted text. The bottom-right corner also had the ‘REP’ of Republic TV, and the name also appeared on the screen behind Lt. Gen Ghai while he spoke.
We checked to see if Republic World had uploaded the speech on their social media handles, and came across the full video on YouTube, which was livestreamed on October 18, under the banner ‘Forces First Conclave.’
We went through the full video and saw that Lt. Gen Rajiv Ghai had spoken at length about Operation Sindoor, commending the military planning and execution by the Indian Army. However, we could not find the part that is now viral section. This led us to think it may have been generated using AI.
On looking closely at the viral clip, we also noticed several inconsistencies in his lip movements as he began to speak, which was c clear sign that it was a deepfake.
We also ran the viral clip through Hive’s AI detection tool. According to the platform, there was a 99.4% possibility of the clip being artificially generated.
Moreover, the Press Information Bureau (PIB) clarified, through an X post, that the AI generated video was being circulated to mislead people and create distrust against the Indian Armed Forces.
🚨 Deepfake Video Alert 🚨
Pakistani propaganda accounts are circulating a digitally altered video of Lieutenant General Rajiv Ghai with false claims suggesting he said that politicization or saffronization of the Indian Armed Forces would damage the morale of the Army… pic.twitter.com/NyOEz2E2af
— PIB Fact Check (@PIBFactCheck) October 23, 2025
Based on these findings, we were able to conclude that the viral video, purportedly showing Lt. Gen Rajiv Ghai speaking about his concerns at the growing influence of ‘saffron politics’ in the Indian Army, was generated using AI and is a deepfake. In reality, the speech, delivered at the ‘Forces First Conclave’ on October 18, did not feature any such comment by the deputy chief of army staff.
claim_text: Lt Gen Rajiv Ghai criticized saffronization of Indian politics, saying that it corrodes the core values of the Indian Army
original_post_text: 🚨Breaking News: Lt Gen Rajiv Ghai (Retd) slams #BJP & #NarendraModi ’s politics, warns that any saffronization or politicization of the Indian Armed Forces will destroy morale. “We will resist all attempts to make the Indian Army subservient to Hindutva.” pic.twitter.com/1HKnvh0x4V ||| 🚨 Deepfake Video Alert 🚨

title: Viral video of gender-divided classroom is from Maharashtra, not Kerala
body_text:
A video depicting a classroom divided by a half wall, with burqa-clad female students seated on one side of the partition and male students on the other, is going viral on the internet. This video is being shared with the claim that the footage shows a college classroom in Kerala.
X user Ocean Jain (@ocjain4) shared the purported video, asserting that it was not from Saudi Arabia, Pakistan, or Bangladesh but from Kerala. (Archive)
यह क्लास का दृश्य पाकिस्तान,सऊदी अरब,यमन या बंगलादेश का नहीं है…..
यह वीडियो केरल के कालेज की एक कक्षा का है…. pic.twitter.com/OSSS2DcvTU— Ocean Jain (@ocjain4) October 22, 2025
The video garnered around 160,000 views.
X user Jitendra Pratap Singh (@jpsin1) also shared the video with similar claims. (Archive)
यह क्लास का दृश्य पाकिस्तान,सऊदी अरब,यमन या बंगलादेश का नहीं है…..
यह वीडियो केरल के कालेज की एक कक्षा का है…. pic.twitter.com/S7jUIIZH9X— 🇮🇳Jitendra pratap singh🇮🇳 (@jpsin1) October 22, 2025
The post received 17,000 views.
Readers should note that X users Ocean Jain and Jitendra Prata Singh have been found to be amplifying communal misinformation regularly. Alt News has debunked several of their claims in the past. Singh is followed by PM Modi on X.
An Instagram page, India.unravelled, shared the video claiming that the video was not from Pakistan but from Kerala, a communist state.
View this post on Instagram
The post has amassed 115,000 views.
It is worth noting that Kerala, a Communist party ruled state which has around 27% Muslim population, is often targeted by the Right wing with anti-Muslim narratives. The video is being shared with a similar intent.
Several social media users have made similar claims. A few of them can be seen in the gallery below.
Fact Check
A reverse image search on a few keyframes of te viral video led us to an Instagram account named aamersrs_mos_academy, which posted the same video on October 10, 2025. The video was uploaded by this account in collaboration with another account named Mukhtar Sir.
View this post on Instagram
According to the Instagram bio of aamersrs_mos_academy, this individual is the director of MOS Academy Nanded.
As we dug further, we could confirm that MOS Academy, a private coaching centre, was not located in Kerala, but in Nanded, Maharashtra. It is not a government college as claimed.
To sum up, the claims that the video depicting a classroom with a partition segregating female students from male students is from a college in Kerala are false. The video is from a private coaching centre in Nanded, Maharashtra.
claim_text: Video shows gender segregated classroom in a college in Kerala

title: Woman beaten up by police in viral video falsely identified as RJD spokesperson Priyanka Bharti
body_text:
A video showing police personnel beating up a woman on the streets even as she screams and cries was viral on social media recently. It is claimed that the woman in the video is Priyanka Bharti, a Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) spokesperson. An image of Bharti is also used in the video.
A verified user named Rajesh Singh posted this video, claiming that the police in Bihar had beaten up RJD spokesperson Priyanka Bharti. (Archived link)
Similarly, a user named Riddhi Sharma shared the video, claiming that the video was from Bihar and that the woman being beaten by the police was RJD spokesperson Priyanka Bharti. (Archived link)
लिल्लाह तेरे होते यह जुर्म हो रहे है।😭
राजद प्रवक्ता प्रियंका भर्ती की दोनों लालटेन 🏮 फोड़ दी बिहार पुलिस ने और पूरा तेल निकालकर डंडे पर रट्टा मारकर फिर उसके टमाटर भी सूजा डाले 😢😢😖
मैं इसकी कड़ी निंदा करती हूँ लिल्ला 😏 pic.twitter.com/0PUFqFnA1s— SanataniRiddhi (@Ridhu417Sharma) September 26, 2025
A user named Kajal Kushwaha also shared the video with the same claim. (Archived link)
Fact Check
Alt News performed a reverse image search using a few frames taken from the viral video. This led us to a post from News18 Kannada dated September 11, 2025. The media outlet reported about an incident in which Maddur police had filed an FIR against a woman named and also beaten her up with sticks. The post includes a link to a News18 Kannada article about the incident.
According to the report, during a Ganesh immersion procession in Maddur, Karnataka, stone-pelting occurred near a mosque, prompting protests from several Hindu organisations, including the BJP. During this incident, police resorted to lathi charge. The woman in the viral video was among the crowd which faced the music. This clearly indicates that the viral video is not from Bihar and the woman seen in the visuals is not RJD spokesperson Priyanka Bharti.
News18 Kannada also published a video report covering the incident:
ಲಾಠಿ ಏಟು ತಿಂದಿದ್ದ ಜ್ಯೋತಿ ಮೇಲೆ ಎಫ್ಐಆರ್! ಮದ್ದೂರು ಪೊಲೀಸರಿಂದ ಸುಮೋಟೋ ಕೇಸ್ ದಾಖಲು#Maddur #Maddurriot #MaddurViolence #MaddurJyothi #Jyothi #KannadaNewshttps://t.co/pX8njSbLtD
— News18 Kannada (@News18Kannada) September 11, 2025
Furthermore, we also came across a report related to the video on the Times Now Kannada website, published on September 11, 2025. The report states that Inspector Shivkumar of Maddur police station had filed an FIR against Jyoti, a resident of Shivapur, Maddur. Jyoti was making offensive remarks against the Muslim community during the protest. The FIR also alleged that Jyoti had abused Karnataka chief minister Siddaramaiah.
To sum up, several social media users shared a video of a woman being lathicharged during a protest in Karnataka, falsely claiming that she was Priyanka Bharti, a spokesperson for the RJD.
claim_text: Woman being beaten up by police in viral video is RJD spokesperson Priyanka Bharti
