The 2022 video shows a mob scaling the building of Hindu man named Ashok Kumar, who had been falsely accused of burning pages of the Quran.
claim_text: Video shows Muslims scaling Hindu homes in Talangana
original_post_text: 🔥ये नजारा अफगानिस्तान,पाकिस्तान का नहीं तेलंगाना का दृश्य है।जो सर तन से जुदा नारे के साथ हिन्दुओं के घरों में जबरन घुस रहें हैं l अपनी सुरक्षा स्वयंम करो वर्ना कश्मीर जैसे हालात हो जाएंगे,कोइ नेता,मिडिया तुम्हें बचाने नहीं आएगी जैसे कश्मीर में हिन्दुओं को कोई बचाने नहीं गया ।।* pic.twitter.com/SD3HegdDxP ||| ये नजारा अफगानिस्तान,पाकिस्तान का नहीं तेलंगाना का दृश्य है।जो सर तन से जुदा नारे के साथ हिन्दुओं के घरों में जबरन घुस रहें हैं l अपनी सुरक्षा स्वयंम करो वर्ना कश्मीर जैसे हालात हो जाएंगे,कोइ नेता,मिडिया तुम्हें बचाने नहीं आएगी pic.twitter.com/RKxYBEOtVP ||| Hyderabad police dispersed a violent mob which was demanding handing over a Hindu sanitary worker accusing him of #blasphemy Police claims the sanitary worker was targeted because of a personal clash with a local resident pic.twitter.com/CnSFLNLqhH

title: ‘Trident in the sky’ image shared by Union govt portal at least 6-yr old, not related to Maha Kumbh 2025
body_text:
On the last day of the 45-day Maha Kumbh Mela in Prayagraj, the Indian Air Force organized an air show. Numerous pictures and videos from the show were shared online. Meanwhile, one such image featuring three aircraft forming the shape of a trident is gathering a lot of traction on social media. It has been claimed that this picture is of the air show held on the last day of the Kumbh Mela.
MyGovIndia, a platform affiliated to the department of citizen participation of the Union government, also posted the viral picture on Instagram. The accompanying caption said this was a scene from the air show organized by the Indian Air Force in Prayagraj on the eve of Maha Shivratri in which three Sukhoi jet planes formed Hindu god Lord Shiva’s trident in the sky. Though this post was deleted later, its archived version is available here.
Retired Major General Harsha Kakar called it the best picture of the year in a post and claimed that three Sukhoi 30 MKI aircraft formed Lord Shiva’s trident in the sky.
Media outlets including Live Hindustan, Nai Dunia, and Times Now also promoted the viral image and claimed that the Indian Air Force had formed a trident in the sky on the last day of the Maha Kumbh.
Several other pages also shared the image with the same claim.
Fact Check
We performed a reverse image search of the viral photo and found it in a tweet by retired Air Marshal Anil Chopra of the Indian Air Force from March 4, 2019. He had tweeted this graphic with a message offering wishes for Maha Shivratri. This makes it clear that this image is not from the air show held on the last day of Maha Kumbh Mela 2025.
Happy Maha Shivratri pic.twitter.com/ePkKe6PdLO
— Aviator Anil Chopra (@Chopsyturvey) March 4, 2019
The above post salutes the Indian Air Force on the occasion of Maha Shivratri and calls them a ‘Maha’ army. A keyword search led us to the same image posted on KOEL’s (Kirloskar Oil Engines Limited) Facebook page on March 4, 2019. The image also features the KOEL logo.
It is to be noted that during air shows, particularly on the Republic Day, the Indian Air Force often forms a trident-like shape in the sky. We found multiple images of this trident formation exercise across several years in the past (2025, 2021, 2020, 2016). The way the trident shape has been formed in all these air shows is completely different from the one seen in the viral image.
The viral image was also shared in 2020 with the claim that the trident shape was formed in the sky by an IAF plane. We found a fact-check report by Vishvas News in 2020 in which the then Public Relations Officer of the Indian Air Force, Anupam Banerjee, had denied the viral image and said that it was not a trident formed by an IAF plane.
claim_text: Image shows trident formed in the sky by IAF planes on the last day of Kumbh Mela 2025
original_post_text: Happy Maha Shivratri pic.twitter.com/ePkKe6PdLO

title: ‘Accident because of collision with truck, no communal angle’: Police, cab driver deny Nazia Elahi Khan’s claim
body_text:
On the morning of February 24, BJP member Nazia Elahi Khan shared a video on X (formerly Twitter) detailing her ordeal while travelling to the Maha Kumbh mela, a holy gathering taking place in Uttar Pradesh’s Prayagraj, from Delhi.
In the video, Khan claimed that a group of Muslims followed her and the two people she was travelling with—her friend Priya Chaturvedi and a 19-year-old girl—from Eta, near Delhi, where she was attending an event. She said that these people, derogatorily referring to them as “mullas,” crashed into her car purposely, causing a major accident. (Archive)
On the way to kumbh I have been targeted by peaceful community 🙏
Help me @myogioffice pic.twitter.com/ZP1cBKWBCZ— Nazia Elahi Khan (सनातनी) (@ElahiNazia1) February 24, 2025
Around 9:30 that morning, Khan, who claims to be a ‘minority leader’ for the BJP and identifies as a ‘Sanatani’ on her X bio, also shared a photo of the wrecked car. She sarcastically remarked that this was the condition in which a “peaceful community” left her car and urged Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath to step in and help her. (Archive)
The car involved in the crash is a cab with a UP registration number (UP79AT52320) and the front-left side is badly damaged, suggesting a collision with something head-on.
This is the condition of car by peaceful community!@myogioffice pic.twitter.com/iZscU0Od1x
— Nazia Elahi Khan (सनातनी) (@ElahiNazia1) February 24, 2025
Several X accounts identifying as pro-Right shared Khan’s video and claims.
Fact Check
The same day, Kanpur Dehat Police, under whose jurisdiction Akbarpur Police Station falls, which is where the accident occured, released a statement contradicting Khan’s.
The police said that the accident was caused because the driver of the cab in which Nazia Elahi Khan was travelling fell asleep. It also urged people to refrain from spreading misinformation on the incident on social media.
— Kanpur Dehat Police (@kanpurdehatpol) February 24, 2025
Here’s a translation of the statement:
“Please be informed that today, February 24, 2025, at approximately 7:00 am, information was received about an accident involving an Ertiga taxi car with registration number UP 79AT 5232 on the highway from Akbarpur towards Kanpur, in front of Anandeshwar Cold Storage within the Akbarpur police station area. Taking immediate cognizance of this information, the local police reached the scene and conducted inquiries. The car driver stated that he fell asleep, causing the accident. When asked to provide a written complaint for further action, he refused to pursue any action and did not submit any complaint. Subsequently, the local police arranged another vehicle and sent everyone to their destination. They praised the police for their good work. Although the incident appears to be an accident, the Circle Officer (CO) of Akbarpur has been directed to conduct a thorough investigation. Please refrain from spreading misleading and baseless information about this incident on social media platforms.”
But Khan took to X and disputed the police’s version. (Archive)
Do you think that I will agree with ur own made agreement!
You was there at that time ?
Your officer leaves me alone with the broken car early morning as bcoz they were on craving for tea !Proof your presence on the place of incident!
Otherwise this matter will go to the…
— Nazia Elahi Khan (सनातनी) (@ElahiNazia1) February 24, 2025
Alt News reached out to officers at the Akbarpur Police Station. They said that when they arrived at the site of the accident to investigate, Nazia Elahi Khan made no mention of being followed by Muslims from Eta, who deliberately caused the accident. If she faced such an issue, she should have raised it that time itself but rather, she took to social media to post such allegations, they said.
The police also said that the driver himself stating that he fell asleep debunks Khan’s claims on social media. They added that if this further escalates, they will have to take action against the BJP member for spreading misinformation.
The police also told Alt News that on Khan’s request they arranged another cab as she wanted to go back to Delhi instead of Prayagraj.
While investigating the incident, Alt News also managed to contact the driver of Khan’s cab, which was involved in the accident. The driver clearly told us that neither was anyone following them from Eta nor was the accident deliberately caused. He also said that before the crash, they stopped briefly for tea and even then he did not notice anything suspicious.
Describing the course of events he said, “A truck overtook us and suddenly slowed down, causing the car to collide with it.” He further clarified, “There is no communal angle to the accident. The claim made by Nazia Elahi Khan is not true”.
He added that after the collision, his vision got blurred and he could note the truck’s registration number. However, he said that he managed to rescue the passengers before blacking out from the impact. However, he added that he did not recall any conversation throughout their journey where Khan mentioned being followed by Muslims from Eta.
While there is a slight contradiction in the police statement and the driver’s version to us regarding how the accident happened (whether or not the driver fell asleep at the wheel), both categorically said that it was an accident and there was no indication of a deliberate attempt. Alt News was not able to independently verify if the driver fell asleep and that’s what caused the collision.
To sum up, the claims made by Nazia Elahi Khan on social media platforms that she was followed by Muslims, who caused the car to get into an accident are misleading and not true. According to the police and the cab driver, there was no communal angle to the road accident. Her cab collided with a truck, causing the wreckage and injuries to passengers. The police specifically said that Khan made no statement on being followed by Muslims when the police first arrived at the spot.
On February 26, the Kanpur Dehat Police shared a statement that Khan had registered a Zero FIR against drivers of 2 white SUVs in the accident case and that investigation on this was ongoing.
claim_text: Nazia Elahi Khan claims she was followed by Muslims who later deliberately crashed into her cab
original_post_text: On the way to kumbh I have been targeted by peaceful community 🙏 Help me @myogioffice pic.twitter.com/ZP1cBKWBCZ ||| This is the condition of car by peaceful community! @myogioffice pic.twitter.com/iZscU0Od1x ||| pic.twitter.com/v0ji3knVBh ||| Do you think that I will agree with ur own made agreement! You was there at that time ? Your officer leaves me alone with the broken car early morning as bcoz they were on craving for tea !

title: ‘Indians should clean up trash’: Viral Trump press conference video is doctored
body_text:
A video of US President Donald Trump being questioned by a reporter on the new customer service bill is doing the rounds on social media. In response to the question, Trump is heard making some derogatory remarks toward Indians. Trump states that multi-million dollar companies would no longer be able to hide behind email-only customer service, and would now have to hire customer service representatives who spoke proper English, not foreigners with thick accents which were hard to understand. He added further that going forward no Indians would take your calls from India. He also remarked that Indians should get jobs to pick up garbage in their country, as garbage is strewn everywhere in India which Indians did not care about as they dumped a lot of garbage and plastic in the rivers.
X user @__Amoxicillin_ shared the video and called it an insult by Trump toward Indians, adding that Modi had projected himself and Indians in the same way.
Disrespectful Comments By Trump For Indians.
This is what modi has projected himself and Indians. pic.twitter.com/bBmQPqJ3rV— Amoxicillin (@__Amoxicillin_) February 22, 2025
Amplifying the same claim, a user named Nasreen Ebrahim tweeted the alleged statement by Trump.
Quote-tweeting Nasreen’s post, senior Congress leader Digvijaya Singh said, “Mr. Narendra Modi, just see what your dear friend Donald Trump thinks of India and Indians. Would you please react? If you cannot, shouldn’t your Minister for External Affairs Dr. S. Jaishankar protest that US President Trump is calling India the most poisonous country in the world?” He later deleted the tweet.
Fact Check
The video appears to be doctored at first glance, as Donald Trump’s voice sounds slightly different. The ticker in the video reads “Sobering Satire”. Apart from this, the name of the news outlet is mentioned as “Faux News”, which is inspired by the logo of the American news channel Fox News and is a distorted and satirical form of the same. ‘Faux’ also means fake or false.
Besides, the last frame of the video shows credit for performance and impressions given to Michael Clive, who is a professional voice artist.
Alt News performed a keyword search on YouTube, which led us to the Sobering Satire channel, the page which had posted the video in question on January 25, 2025. The description of the video clearly states that the clip is made for satirical purposes. In addition, the video also appears in the Altered or synthetic content category on YouTube. The ‘About’ section of this channel also states that it makes satire videos.
When we performed another keyword search using related terms on Google, we could not find any recent statement by Donald Trump in which he had made derogatory comments about India as seen in the viral video.
Next, we performed a reverse image search using a frame taken from the viral video. This led us to a video of Donald Trump’s statement shared by an American journalist, which had the logo of “Fox News”. In this video, the video’s background and Trump’s attire are similar to the viral video, but his audio and voice are different.
Trump on Canada: We don’t need their fuel or energy or oil and gas we don’t need anything that they have. pic.twitter.com/8xLaIQq1Or
— Acyn (@Acyn) January 10, 2025
We also came across a longer version of the press conference that matches the viral video. Donald Trump has not talked about India anywhere here. In addition, Trump’s voice in this video is also different from the viral video. In other words, this video of Donald Trump was edited and the audio was added to it with lip-sync using the help of artificial intelligence.
To sum up, social media users and Congress leader Digvijaya Singh shared a doctored video of US President Donald Trump believing it to be true, and falsely claimed that he had made derogatory comments about Indians.
claim_text: Video shows Trump making derogatory comments about Indians in press conference
original_post_text: Disrespectful Comments By Trump For Indians. This is what modi has projected himself and Indians. pic.twitter.com/bBmQPqJ3rV ||| Trump on Canada: We don’t need their fuel or energy or oil and gas we don’t need anything that they have. pic.twitter.com/8xLaIQq1Or

title: Viral claim that ‘European research survey’ labelled India biggest source of terrorism in Asia is baseless, made up
body_text:
Claims that a European research survey has declared India the largest source of terrorism in Asia are viral on social media.
X account @NavCom24 shared this claim on February 21 with a picture of Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi with a tricolour in the background.
The post reached over 104,000 users.
Note that this X account is currently withheld in Indias.
Another X account, @GlobalEyeInfo or The Global Eye, which describes itself as an “independent News Agency on X” shared the same viral claim. However, like the previous user, this account too did not mention which European body had conducted this research. (Archive)
BREAKING: 🇮🇳 India declared the largest source of terrorism in Asia by a European research survey. pic.twitter.com/3QOs7xzY1E
— The Global Eye (@GlobalEyeInfo) February 21, 2025
The same day, X user @TamzidSalehin, shared a similar assertion, calling it “Breaking” news, alongside a morphed picture of PM Modi with red demonic eyes. (Archive)
🚨BREAKING: India has been declared the largest source of terrorism in Asia by a European research survey. pic.twitter.com/LGDMGymmh8
— Tamzid (@TamzidSalehin) February 21, 2025
Many other accounts too posted similar claims.
Note that most of the accounts mentioned here have content that is either pro-Pakistan or pro-Bangladesh and often anti-India.
Fact Check
Alt News conducted multiple keyword searches but found no report or any credible evidence supporting such claims.
On February 19, wire agency Press Trust of India had published a report citing India’s permanent representative to the UN Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish as saying: “Pakistan is the global epicentre of terrorism harboring more than 20 UN-listed terrorist entities and providing state support to cross-border terrorism.”
The ambassador goes on to say that it is very ironical that Pakistan praises itself for being at the forefront of the fight against terrorism whereas India has been a victim of terror acts perpetrated by the country through Jaish-e-Mohammed and Harkat Ul Mujahidin among others.
While investigating these claims, we came across the 2024 Global Terrorism Index (GTI) report by the Institute for Economics and Peace (IEP) published in february last year.
The IEP report, which looks at global trends and the overall impact of terrorism, ranked Pakistan at the fourth place among 163 countries for the year 2023. India is ranked fourteenth.
These rankings are based on GTI scores, which considers not only deaths but also incidents, hostages and injuries from terrorism in a particular year weighted over a five-year period.
According the 2024 report, Pakistan had moved up three places to fourth on the Index, “its worst
place since 2016.” The neighbouring country, along with Nigeria and Somalia, has been among those most consistently hit by terrorism since 2011. The report did not say much about India
To sum up, claims that India has been labelled the largest source of terrorism in Asia by a European research survey are bogus and unsubstantiated. There has been no recent ‘European research survey’ that says this nor has any credible European survey agency made such assertions.
claim_text: India declared the largest source of terrorism in Asia by a European research survey
original_post_text: BREAKING: 🇮🇳 India declared the largest source of terrorism in Asia by a European research survey. pic.twitter.com/3QOs7xzY1E ||| 🚨BREAKING: India has been declared the largest source of terrorism in Asia by a European research survey. pic.twitter.com/LGDMGymmh8

title: Hindutva outfit leaders misidentified as father of boy accused of abetting KIIT student’s suicide
body_text:
Odisha’s Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology (KIIT) has come under fire after an international student from Nepal allegedly killed herself after being harassed by another male student. The institute administration came under further scrutiny after reports and videos and showed staff misbehaving and manhandling Nepalese students protesting against the authorities for not intervening in the case of their fellow student Prakriti Lamsal.
Lamsal, a student from Nepal, was found dead in her hostel room on Sunday, February 16. She was allegedly harassed by another student named Advik Srivastava. Protesting students claim that the college, despite knowing about the harassment, did not step in to protect Lamsal and instead took a soft stance against Srivastava. The Bhubaneshwar police, where KIIT is located, has registered a case and booked Srivastava for abetment of suicide.
Amidst this, images of two men with obvious links to Hindutva organisations have gone viral on social media. Users claimed these images were of Advik Srivastava’s father, Manoj Srivastava, and that his political reach was the reason for KIIT’s inaction.
One such image shows an image of a man in a saffron stole juxtaposed with another picture of him from his Facebook profile where his name—ManojK Srivastava—can be read. A day after Lamsal’s body was found, X account @manav_shah2000 shared the screenshot of a Reddit post, where someone had put together the two photos. Through the post, the user also implied that the institute was protecting Srivastava because his father was a politician. (Archive)
Wonder why this College is protecting Advik Srivastava??
Answer: 👇 https://t.co/9gb59ZVIXt pic.twitter.com/e0wIvfLWLw
— Manav Shah (@manav_shah2000) February 17, 2025
Another collage, shared by X account @_JoshNeverStops on February 20, showed images of a man standing with Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath and prominent BJP politican Smriti Irani. The user claimed that the man in the photos was Advik Srivastava’s father Manoj Srivastava, an influential BJP politician from Lucknow. (Archive)
He is Manoj Shrivastava, an influential BJP politician from Lucknow and the father of Advik Srivastava.#KIITUniversity #JusticeForPrakriti pic.twitter.com/K2PwmMdXlJ
— KeepitDope (@_JoshNeverStops) February 20, 2025
Fact Check
We fact checked the two claims and images separately and here’s what we found:
Photo 1
To check the veracity of the photo, Alt News looked for the Facebook profile shown in the image. We found one profile that matched the name and image as the viral post. According to this, Srivastava is a resident of Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh and heads a Hindutva outfit, Bharatiya Kesariya Vahini.
Alt News then contacted this person who categorically denied being linked to the Advik Srivastava accused in the KIIT incident. This man said he did have a son, but he was 16 years old and still in school.
“I am not that Manoj Srivastava. My pictures are being misused. I am being misidentified. I have only one son, who is too young to attend an engineering college, he is still in school and is in 12th standard,” this person said.
He added that he was the president of Bharatiya Kesariya Vahini since 2019 and is also a member of the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh. He claimed that he has been involved in social activism for some time and was active in politics as a student. He told Alt News that he has been bullied and subjected to much abuse on all his social media accounts ever since he was misidentified as the accused’s father.
This person also posted a video on his Facebook account dismissing claims that he was associated with Advik Srivastava.
उड़ीसा में छात्रा की मौत में संदेह के तौर पर पकड़े गए अद्विक श्रीवास्तव को मीडिया द्वारा गलत प्रचारित किया जा रहा है कि वह मेरा पुत्र है जो बिल्कुल गलत है मेरे नाम व हमारे वर्षों सामाजिक प्रतिष्ठा को धूमिल किए जाने का असफल प्रयास किया जा रहा है। जो लोग अगर गलतफहमी में मेरे नाम व फोटो का प्रयोग कर रहे हैं तो वह तुरंत खंडन करे और मेरा नाम , फोटो हटाए ।
धन्यवादPosted by Manojk Srivastava on Tuesday 18 February 2025
Photo 2:
While the man in the second photo is Manoj Srivastava, former district vice president of BJP Ayodhya, a post on his facebook profile shows that he is not the father of the KIIT accused.
On his Facebook profile, this man had shared a post for his son Akshat Srivastava on the latter’s birthday. While investigating, we found that his son is the MD and CEO at V.M. Auto Motor Ayodhya.
Alt News then reached out to Akshat Srivastava, who confirmed that he is the only son of the Manoj Srivastava in the picture and runs his own business. He added that his family has been residing in Lucknow for a long time and said that the claims linking his father to the KIIT accused is absolutely baseless.
Manoj Srivastava too denied any connection to Advik Srivastava. “I have only one son, Akshat Srivastava, and a daughter. I was born in Ayodhya and still live here with my family. I have no familial ties to Advik. Any claim suggesting otherwise is false,” he told Alt News.
Why Manoj Srivastava?
But Manoj Srivastava’s name did not get dragged into the incident out of thin air. Turns out, it is indeed the name of the accused’s father. But social media users misidentified him and circulated images of wrong people with the same name. Note that both Manoj and Srivastava are common first and last names in the country.
To be sure, Alt News spoke to a journalist based in Bhubaneswar and sourced a press note by the local police. A summary of the FIR shows the name of the accused as Advik Srivastava and his father’s name as Manoj Srivastava. They are residents of Lucknow. The FIR also says that the accused was nabbed while he was trying to flee the state.
Here’s a translation of the gist of the FIR:
“Infocity Police, following a complaint from the complainant, Sidhant Sigdel, investigated the case and arrested the accused and sent him to court.
Reportedly, the victim was from Nepal and was studying at a reputed educational institution in Bhubaneswar and residing in the institution’s hostel. Two years prior, she became acquainted with the accused. Subsequently, the accused secretly took some photos and videos of her on his mobile phone. Due to some reason, a dispute arose between them. As a result, the issue was resolved twice with the help of the International Relations Offic under the educational institution, but there was no change in the accused’s behavior. Instead, the accused started harassing her again. The victim was under severe mental distress due to this incident. On 16.02.2025, there was another quarrel between them. That afternoon, her classmates found her hanging from the fan in her room. They immediately rescued her and took her to KIMS Hospital, but the doctor declared her dead.
Following a complaint, Infocity Police investigated the case and managed to arrest the accused near the airport while he was trying to flee Osidha. At that time, police also seized a mobile phone and a laptop from him. During interrogation, the accused confessed to his crime. Further investigation into the matter is ongoing.”
He has been remanded to 14 days of judicial custody.
Alt News reached out to Infocity Bhubaneswar police who told us that according to the accused, his father Manoj Srivastava is a civil engineer and contractor in Lucknow. They had no information regarding his father’s involvement with any political party.
To sum up, the men in both these viral image have been misidentified as Advik Srivastava’s father.
claim_text: Photos identifying father of Advik Srivastava, accused for abetting Prakriti Lamsal's suicide in KIIT
original_post_text: Wonder why this College is protecting Advik Srivastava?? ||| He is Manoj Shrivastava, an influential BJP politician from Lucknow and the father of Advik Srivastava. #KIITUniversity #JusticeForPrakriti pic.twitter.com/K2PwmMdXlJ

title: Delhi quake: Republic ran unrelated videos, including one from Japan, in its reportage
body_text:
A 4.0-magnitude earthquake shook New Delhi at 5:36 am on February 17. Within hours, media organisations as well as social media users started sharing a CCTV clip from Pakistan claiming it to be a frightening video depicting the New Delhi earthquake.
On February 17 itself, Republic World published a short video on its YouTube channel with the caption ‘Delhi-NCR earthquake: First videos shared by residents are quite disturbing’. This video contains several different clips. In one of them, water is seen spilling out of a bathtub due to the effects of the earthquake. (Archived link)
X-user @theinformant_x, which claims to be an “independent and libertarian journalist covering global news”, shared three videos with a screenshot of a report of the National Center for Seismology on the earthquake. The video of water spilling out of a bathtub was one of them. So was the CCTV footage from Pakistan mentioned above, which Alt News has already debunked. (Archived link)
❗️🇮🇳 – Strong tremors were felt in Delhi this morning.
The shaking continued for several seconds, causing widespread panic as people rushed out of their homes.
The earthquake was measured at a magnitude of 4.0 on the Richter scale, with its epicenter located in Delhi. pic.twitter.com/ZSSAAOk3sm
— 🔥🗞The Informant (@theinformant_x) February 17, 2025
Fact Check
Alt News performed a reverse image search using frames taken from the short video by Republic World. We found a video of water gushing out of a bathtub uploaded on a YouTube channel called RM Video on February 15, 2021. It is also mentioned that “water was seen gushing out of the bathtub due to shaking during the earthquake in Japan”. (Archived link)
This makes it evident that the video of the bathtub is at least four-year old and has no connection with the recent earthquake in Delhi.
Further investigation led us to find that the bathtub video was not the only one Republic ran falsely in its reports on the February 17 Delhi quake.
The video of a ceiling fan swaying during a quake — anther clip that Republic ran — was found on the X timeline of ANI Hindi News. It was shared on August 20, 2024. The caption says that this is a video showing a 4.9-magnitude earthquake in Jammu and Kashmir. (Archived link)
In addition, the video of the chandelier shaking, again part of Republic’s Delhi quake reportage, was shared by ANI (Asian News International) on X on January 7, 2025 when it reported on tremors were felt in Sheohar, Bihar, during a 7.1-intensity earthquake. (Archived link)
TV9 Bharatvarsh also published a report on the Delhi earthquake and used a screenshot of the chandelier video as an image from an earthquake in Bihar. (Archived link)
To sum up, news channel Republic World falsely ran at least three unrelated videos in its reportage of the February 17 New Delhi earthquake.
claim_text: Footage shows scenes from February 17 Delhi earthquake
original_post_text: ❗️🇮🇳 – Strong tremors were felt in Delhi this morning.

title: Woman wielding sword in viral video is not Delhi CM Rekha Gupta
body_text:
After the BJP recently returned to power in Delhi asaembly after 27 years, the party’s newly elected MLA from Shalimar Bagh Rekha Gupta took oath as the chief minister of Delhi on February 20, 2025. Against this backdrop, many social media users were seen sharing old videos of Rekha Gupta. One of these clips shows a woman wielding a sword. Social media users claim that this is an old video of Rekha Gupta, who was once an RSS worker and worked with the ABVP, and has been associated with BJP for a long time.
Verified X handle ‘@Ra_Bies’ shared the video claiming it to be of Rekha Gupta. The video has garnered more than 28,000 views at the time of this article being writing. (Archived version)
She is Rekha Gupta, CM of Delhi and watching this old video of her should make us proud. I request Kejriwal to hide in some rathole to escape her fury pic.twitter.com/QWjr1D7t7q
— Ra_Bies 3.0 (@Ra_Bies) February 21, 2025
Verified X user Komal Shekhawat also shared the video with a similar claim. The tweet was later deleted. (Archived link of the X post)
Several X and Facebook users have shared the clip claiming it to be of Rekha Gupta.
Fact Check
Alt News found that several users had replied to the posts with the videos in circulation claiming that the woman in the video was actually Payal Jadhav. Keeping this in mind, we looked up Jadhav and found that she was a Marathi actress and had worked in several Marathi films and shows.
This video was posted by Payal Jadhav on Instagram on February 19, 2025. Payal paid tribute to Chhatrapati Shivaji in the caption. She also tagged ‘Savyasachi Gurukul’ for martial-arts training. ‘Savyasachi Gurukul’ is a centre established for training in martial arts in Vengurla, Kolhapur.
View this post on Instagram
At the same time, Marathi entertainment YouTube channel ‘Rajshri Marathi Showbuzz’ also uploaded this video claiming it to be of Payal Jadhav. Jadhav has also posted this video on Facebook.
To sum up, a video of a Marathi actress Payal Jadhav wielding a sword was falsely shared as an old video of chief minister of Delhi, Rekha Gupta.
claim_text: Video shows Delhi CM Rekha Gupta
original_post_text: She is Rekha Gupta, CM of Delhi and watching this old video of her should make us proud. I request Kejriwal to hide in some rathole to escape her fury pic.twitter.com/QWjr1D7t7q

title: Mohammad Shami, Mohammad Kaif, and Yogi Adityanath’s Kumbh posturing in UP assembly
body_text:
In the speech in Uttar Pradesh assembly on February 19, in which chief minister Yogi Adityanath rejected the report by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) on the water at the Ganga Triveni sangam in Prayagraj being currently ‘unsafe’ for bathing, he also vociferously refuted allegations made by various opposition leaders about the UP government’s ‘failure’ to manage and organise the Kumbh Mela properly.
In this context, Adityanath claimed in the assembly that Indian cricketer Mohammad Shami had taken a holy dip at the Maha Kumbh. He said, “There is no basis in the allegations of discrimination at the Maha Kumbh. Cricketer Mohammed Shami also took a holy dip.”
Fact Check
We conducted multiple keyword searches but found no news reports regarding Indian cricketer Mohammad Shami’s visit to the 2025 Prayagraj Maha Kumbh or his participation in the ritualistic dip. We went through Shami’s social media timelines as well, but we did not find anything regarding his visit to the Kumbh Mela.
Readers would agree that with the kind of national and international media attention the Kumbh has received, such an incident as a star cricketer’s visit to the Mela would have surely made into news reports.
Moreover, we found that on January 11, 2025, India announced their 15-member squad for the five-match T20 series against England with fast bowler Mohammad Shami included in the lineup. The series began at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata on January 22. The Indian team arrived in Kolkata on January 18. After the concluding match at Wankhede stadium in Mumbai on February 2, India played three ODIs in Nagpur, Cuttack and Ahmedabad on February 6, 9 and 12. Subsequently, the team departed for Dubai on Saturday, February 15, to participate in the ICC Men’s Champions Trophy 2025. Shami is a part of the squad for this tournament as well.
The 2025 Praygraj Maha Kumbh started from January 13 and will conclude on February 26.
The timeline of the events suggests that Mohammad Shami has been actively engaged in national team commitments almost non-stop since January 18, making it unlikely that he visited the 2025 Prayagraj Maha Kumbh during this period. And as mentioned earlier, there is no media report or publicly available information of him visiting the Kumbh.
Akhilesh’s Name-Change Quip
Hours after Adityanath’s speech in the UP assembly, Samajwadi Party leader Akhilesh Yadav shared a cryptic post on X. It said, “अब क्या क्रिकेटर का भी नाम बदल दिया?” (Has he how changed the name of a cricketer?”)
अब क्या क्रिकेटर का भी नाम बदल दिया?
— Akhilesh Yadav (@yadavakhilesh) February 19, 2025
While the tweet did not say anything else, it was quickly interpreted as Akhilesh taking a dig at Adityanath for the latter having confused former Indian cricketer Mohammad Kaif with Mohammad Shami. Several media houses claimed the same. Here are a few headlines:
Did Mohammad Kaif Visit the Kumbh?
No. Well, likely not.
Former Indian cricketer Mohammad Kaif shared a photo of himself plunging into the Yamuna in Prayagraj nearly two weeks before the Maha Kumbh began on January 13. On December 29, 2024, Kaif posted a video on X where he could be seen taking a plunge into the river. He wrote in the caption that “I learnt how to swim in this very same Yamuna”.
Abe isi Jamuna ji mein tairaki seekha hoon 😀 #sangam #prayagraj pic.twitter.com/yVs67yJMWb
— Mohammad Kaif (@MohammadKaif) December 29, 2024
Just like in the case of Shami, there is no media report or publicly available information of Kaif taking a dip at the Kumbh Mela.
So, the claims by a section of the media that it was Mohammad Kaif who had taken a dip at the Kumbh and Adityanath confused him with Mohammad Shami, is misleading, to say the least, if not factually incorrect.
AI Images of ‘Shami at Kumbh’
Alt News would like to inform its readers that several AI-generated pictures of Mohammad Shami with tennis star Sania Mirza are currently viral on social media platforms. In one such picture, Shami is seen wearing a saffron stole, and the caption reads, “Sania Mirza with Mohammad Shami in Maha Kumbh ai pic”.
In another picture, Shami has a tilak (sandalwood-paste marking used by Hindus on auspicious occasions) on his forehead and he is again seen with Sania Mirza. Even this picture has a similar caption.
The Facebook page that has uploaded these pictures mentions that they are AI-generated. We are not attaching the images here to stop their amplification further.
claim_text: Indian cricketer Mohammed Shami took a holy dip at Prayagraj Maha Kumbh
original_post_text: अब क्या क्रिकेटर का भी नाम बदल दिया? ||| Abe isi Jamuna ji mein tairaki seekha hoon 😀 #sangam #prayagraj pic.twitter.com/yVs67yJMWb

title: No, Railways has not reintroduced senior citizen discount scheme; viral message is false
body_text:
Of late, a claim pertaining to the senior citizen discount scheme in Indian Railways is gathering a lot of traction on social media. As per the claim, the Railways has announced a big relief in fares for senior citizen passengers. It also states that from February 15, 2025, women will be provided a 50% discount while men would be granted a 40% discount on rail tickets. It is also claimed that this scheme was discontinued during the Coronavirus pandemic, and is now being restarted.
X user Sanjay Wadekar amplified the message with similar claims, explaining the benefits of the Railway Senior Citizen Concession Scheme. His X profile shows that he is allegedly a BJP worker and is also associated with the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh. (Archived link)
15 फरवरी से सीनियर सिटिज़न को मिलेगा 50% छूट का लाभ, अब लंबी यात्रा होगी सस्ती
February 13, 2025 Devanshu
भारतीय रेलवे ने सीनियर सिटीजन (वरिष्ठ नागरिक) यात्रियों के लिए बड़ी राहत की घोषणा की है। 15 फरवरी 2025 से, महिलाओं को 50% और पुरुषों को 40% की छूट दी जाएगी। यह सुविधा विशेष…
— Sanjay wadekar BJP (@Sanjaywadekar9) February 13, 2025
Instagram account ‘Times Applied Trends’ also promoted the claim about the launch of the discount scheme for senior citizens. (Archived link)
Several other websites such as Appear News and ‘UpexcisePortal.in‘ also amplified this claim. (Archived link 1, link 2)
Fact Check
Alt News dug deeper into these viral claims. However, we could not find any recent press releases or news reports to corroborate the message being circulated currently.
The press notes uploaded on the IRCTC website on March 20, 2020 state that the ‘Senior Citizen Concession’ has been withdrawn with effect from that day. In addition, the Railway Board circular dated March 19, 2020 mentions that in view of the health advisory issued regarding COVID-19, concessions in passenger fare for all categories of passengers, except four categories of Divyangjans and 11 categories of patients, had been withdrawn till further orders.
According to a story by ABP Live on August 4, 2024, as per PTI inputs, the Union railway minister was asked about the earlier concession on fares provided to senior citizens. Ashwini Vaishnav, while answering in Parliament, said that in 2022-23, the Indian Railways had given a subsidy of Rs. 57,000 crore to provide affordable facilities to the passengers. All passengers were receiving its benefits. However, he did not mention any concession given to senior citizens. He has also argued earlier that there was no plan to provide an exclusive discount to senior citizens.
According to the information available on ERail.in, too, the senior citizen quota has been discontinued. The computerised passenger reservation system (PRS) has a provision to automatically allocate lower berths to senior citizens and female passengers aged 45 years and above, even if no option is given, provided space is available at the time of booking. A combined quota of two lower berths per coach has been fixed in Sleeper, AC 3 Tier and AC 2 Tier classes for senior citizens travelling alone, female passengers aged 45 years and above, and pregnant women.
The Union Budget 2025-26 also does not mention any scheme or policy that provides concessions to senior citizens travelling by train or any plans for the resumption of the scheme.
To sum up, concessions for senior citizens in railway tickets were discontinued in March 2020 during COVID-19, and the scheme has not been restored till now. In other words, the viral message on social media is false.
claim_text: Senior citizens' discount scheme revived by indian Railways
original_post_text: February 13, 2025 Devanshu

title: Zee, NDTV, Jagran, Dainik Bhaskar, ABP, others ran Pakistan quake footage as visuals of Delhi tremors
body_text:
A 4.0 magnitude earthquake with its epicentre near Dhaula Kuan shook Delhi at 5:36 am on February 17. Within hours, social media users and media organizations shared a CCTV clip claiming it to be a terrifying video of the tremors.
NDTV shared this video on X (formerly Twitter) and said that the tremors were so strong that the ground kept shaking for several seconds. This tweet was later deleted but its archived version can be accessed here.
Zee News Hindi ran the same CCTV footage as scenes from the Delhi earthquake. (Archived link)
Media houses like Lokmat Times, Jansatta, English Jagran, Dainik Bhaskar used the same video or its screengrab in their reports on the Delhi earthquake. These reports include a tweet by X user @mooniesssoobin in which he described it as a CCTV video of his house. (Archived link 1, link 2, link 3, link 4, link 5)
Dainik Bhaskar journalists Manish Mishra and Shailesh Verma also shared this video on X claiming it to be the Delhi earthquake incident. (Archived link 1, link 2)
Nagaland satellite channel NL TV and Bengali daily Anandabazar Patrika’s official handle shared the clip on Instagram as footage of the Delhi earthquake. (Archived link 1, link 2)
Fact Check
We observed that the viral CCTV clip had a date and time clearly mentioned on the top right corner of the footage: February 15, 2025 and 22:48.18 hours. According to the National Center for Seismology, the earthquake in Delhi occurred on February 17, 2025 at 05:36 hours. (Archived link)
We performed a reverse image search of the key frames of the viral CCTV video and found a post by Pakistani X user Muhammad Abdullah Hashmi. This post dated February 16 has the hashtag Islamabad mentioned and the user has described it as a video taken from his house.
Just Look at the Blast and Wave it was something else still thinking about it
My Home CCTV video #earthquake #Islamabad pic.twitter.com/vpnTNZyad4— Muhammad Abdullah Hashmi (@PhantomriderxX) February 15, 2025
Upon further investigation, we also came across a video report by Pakistani YouTube channel Samaa TV dated February 15, 2025. According to the report, earthquake tremors were felt in Pakistan’s Rawalpindi and Islamabad at 10:48 pm on February 15, 2025.
To sum up, CCTV footage of an earthquake in Pakistan on February 15 was shared and telecast by the India media as visuals of the tremors in Delhi on February 17 without any kind of verification. It was particularly bizarre because the footage had the date and time mentioned on it, which did not stop the outlets from using it falsely in their bulletins or social media posts.
claim_text: Footage shows Delhi earthquake
original_post_text: Just Look at the Blast and Wave it was something else still thinking about it My Home CCTV video #earthquake #Islamabad pic.twitter.com/vpnTNZyad4

title: Rajasthan police’s claims that Elvish Yadav video ‘old and AI-generated’ false
body_text:
YouTuber Elvish Yadav’s vlog on his visit to Rajasthan has sparked a major controversy. The video, titled “Rajasthan Me Full Protocol” shot during his recent visit to the state and uploaded on February 10, has left many questioning the workings of the Rajasthan Police in matters that are in no way connected to the state government.
The vlog shows Yadav, who was in Rajasthan to shoot a music video, travelling in a car with Krishnavardhan Singh Khachariyawas, son of former Rajasthan minister Pratap Singh Khachariyawas. However, the car, driven by the former minister’s son, is escorted by a vehicle belonging to the state police. Khachariyawas can be heard explaining that the police escort vehicle will change as they pass through different areas. This had many wondering why the police, funded by the state, was being used for such things.
Why the video has raised alarm
- Special police facilities provided to YouTuber Elvish Yadav and ex-minister’s son
Several frames in the vlog show at least one police escort vehicle accompanying the car driven by the former minister’s son and Yadav. From the video, we were able to determine that a Rajasthan Police Bolero (Chetak-14) was used in the beginning. A white Rajasthan Police Bolero with a yellow registration number (RJ14TF5324) was also used while another police Bolero with a white license plate accompanied them after they returned from the music video shoot. So, that much is clear and certain that the Rajasthan Police did escort Elvish Yadav. At the end of the vlog, Khachariyawas can also be heard saying that a police vehicle would drop Yadav to Gurgaon and a police vehicle leads his convoy from Jaipur to Gurgaon.
According to protocol, police escorts are provided only in certain circumstances. So, them being deployed here doesn’t make sense.
- Use of beacons in private vehicle
 In the video, a private vehicle carrying Yadav and the former minister’s son has a beacon, which is used several times. For some years now, the use of beacons and sirens is only permitted for certain class of vehicles and used in emergencies. It is illegal for private vehicles to use it. However, police personnel present there do not intervene or object to this, which raises questions about their commitment to upholding the law and whether they are trying to promote ‘VIP culture’.
- No payment at toll plaza
 At one point in the video, when the convoy passes through a toll plaza, a policeman in the escort vehicle signals to the employees at the toll booth to let Yadav’s vehicle pass. So, the car carrying the YouTuber, with the siren on, passes through without paying the toll, flouting the law.
- Violation of traffic rules
 In the video, the former minister’s son can be seen violating traffic rules, including driving on the wrong side of the road in Transport Nagar and using his influence to get the way cleared in front of the police. Such things not only undermine law but also raise questions on accountability and misuse of influence. Here’s the full video:
Rajasthan Police’s Denial
After the video went viral, Jaipur Police took to X (formerly Twitter) to clear the air. Responding to journalists, they claimed that several videos were edited using artificial intelligence to spread misleading information. In a likely attempt to pressure scribes, they warned that the cyber crime department would take strict action against those spreading misinformation. As a result, one of the journalists, who first drew attention to the incident, deleted their tweet.
विभिन्न वीडियोज को AI Tools की सहायता से एडिट करके भ्रामक रूप से खबर प्रकाशित करने वालों के ख़िलाफ़ सख़्त कार्यवाही जयपुर पुलिस के साइबर थाना द्वारा प्रारंभ की गई है।
— Jaipur Police (@jaipur_police) February 10, 2025
In a press conference, Jaipur Police Commissioner Biju George Joseph also denied the allegations, saying that the video in question was not recent, calling it an attempt to misrepresent old footage as current to damage the reputation of the police. He indicated that action would be taken against Elvish Yadav.
Jaipur में यूट्यूबर एल्विश यादव मामले पर बोले पुलिस कमिश्नर बीजू जार्ज जोसेफ | Rajasthan News#FINVideo #RajasthanWithFirstIndia #JaipurNews #RajasthanNews #BijuGeorgeJoseph @jaipur_police pic.twitter.com/VXlnqHjrFD
— First India News (@1stIndiaNews) February 11, 2025
Meanwhile, Additional Commissioner of Police Rameshwar Singh said that police escorts are provided only under specific conditions and claimed that Rajasthan Police made no such arrangements for the YouTuber.
Is the Police Lying?
Clips of the incident shared by journalists have been taken from Elvish Yadav’s vlog itself. Contrary to police’s claims, these clips have not been edited using AI. Also, the police has not provided any substantial evidence to show that nothing such as this happened or that AI was indeed used.
Elvish Yadav’s vlog has enough evidence to show that police escort vehicles were there so the police’s denial of is clearly not true. Ironically, it is titled “Rajasthan me full protocol” to show the kind of police ‘assistance’ Yadav received. Instances of police vehicles driving ahead of Yadav’s car throughout the video are not coincidences. Remember that the police also helped Yadav’s vehicle pass through toll plazas without paying, refuting the police’s claims that no escort was provided.
Police Commissioner Biju George Joseph saying that the videos are old is also misleading. There are several clues in Elvish Yadav’s vlog to indicate that the video is indeed from his recent visit to Jaipur.
For instance, in the vlog, Elvish Yadav says that he was in Jaipur for a music video shoot. The video captures his journay and visit to Lohagarh Fort Resort and Sambhar Lake where the music video is being shot. In the vlog, Prince Narula also makes an appearance and is seen donning a black kurta along with Yadav. In an Instagram story on February 8, Narula shared a video where he says that he was at the Lohagarh Fort Resort to shoot a music video with Yadav and Pranjal Dahiya. We contacted the resort and confirmed that Elvish Yadav was there for the shoot on February 8 and 9. This contradicts the claims made by the police that the vlog shows old footage.
Apart from Yadav’s vlog, many social media users also shared pictures and videos from the shoot, in which Yadav and Narula are seen wearing the same clothes as in the vlog. These details make it clear that the vlog was of their recent visit to Jaipur and not old.
Also, on February 10, former Rajasthan minister Pratap Singh Khachariyawas posted a picture with Elvish Yadav on X and said that he met the YouTuber at his Jaipur residence. In this picture, Yadav is wearing a black pullover jacket with blue stripes, which is the same one he is seen wearing in the vlog while being escorted by the police along with the former minister’s son Harshvardhan Khachariyawas.
Lastly, in the vlog, Pratap Khachariyawas’s son is seen in the same clothes — a t-shirt with thick white stripes — when the police escort vehicles are there and when him and Yadav visit a hospital. Here, the camera takes a close-up shot of a patient health monitor, in which the date, February 10, is visible. This further cements the fact that the vlog was recorded on February 10 and the police claims that the footage is old are baseless.
What former minister Pratap Khachariyawas and his son said
Denying any involvement in the case, former minister Pratap Singh Khachariyawas said that Elvish Yadav came to meet him often and that as a politician he interacts with many. The Congress leader further added that neither him nor his son requested to be escorted by the police and since his government was not in power in the state, he had no idea why a police vehicle was escorting Elvish Yadav and his son. Passing the baton, he said that people should question the Rajasthan government, under the BJP, or Elvish Yadav why a police vehicle was escorting them.
Rajasthan Police’s cover-up FIR
The Rajasthan Police has filed an FIR against Elvish Yadav for allegedly spreading misleading information and damaging their reputation. However, it seems that the FIR is a strategic act to save the image of the police and avoid a media trial.
Yadav’s vlog has ample evidence that can aid an independent investigation. The police also have the necessary resources to examine CCTV footage from various locations. But an unsatisfactory story has been presented to the media, along with attempts to obfuscate the facts, which reflects lack of genuine intent to actually pursue a comprehensive and thorough investigation.
claim_text: Video of Elvish Yadav being treated like VIP, escorted by police vehicles old, edited using AI

title: Bizarre wedding decision of Saudi man 12 years ago reshared with AI-generated image
body_text:
An image of a man sitting with four burqa-clad women is being circulated on social media with claims that the man from Saudi Arabia married a principal, a teacher, a supervisor, and a student from the same school.
An X user, Raghu (@IndiaTales7), posted this picture on February 12, 2025, with the caption “Some Crazy Images from the Past”, and created a thread with 26 other pictures with separate explanatory captions. This particular photo topped the list. (archive)
Some Crazy images from the Past.🧵
— Raghu (@IndiaTales7) February 12, 2025
The post has garnered over 6 million views so far.
On January 4, 2025, X user The Jaipur Dialogues (@JaipurDialogues), which shares misinformation and communal propaganda on a regular basis, shared the same image on X and Facebook along with a caption, “Islam For You!!” (archive)
Islam For You!! pic.twitter.com/5D6ivJNpDq
— The Jaipur Dialogues (@JaipurDialogues) January 4, 2025
The X post has almost 1 Lakh views.
Several other social media users have shared the image along with the claim.
Fact Check
A closer look at the image reveals certain discrepancies in the picture. The eyes of both the women on the man’s left seem/ appear unnatural to say the least, with their line of sight defocused and distorted.
The hands of at least four of the five individuals, and the fingers in particular, are distorted in the image.
Readers should note that distorted fingers is an error typical of AI-generated images.
Next, we used Sightengine, an AI image detector tool, to verify the photo. It showed a 99% possibility of the image being AI-generated.
We tried a few other AI-detection tools such as Hive Moderation, Brandwell , Google Detect AI images, and aiimagedetector.org. All of them showed high probability that the image was not authentic and likely to be AI-generated.
Now, to verify the authenticity of the claim, we performed a relevant keyword search that led us to several news articles, like the Saudi Gazette, Hurriyet Daily News and more back from the year 2012. The Independent published an article on November 21, 2012, wrote that a 50-year-old man from Saudi had made a bizarre decision and married a pupil, a teacher, and the principal all from the same school. He also has a fourth wife, who worked as an educational supervisor, mostly supervising the school that her husband’s other wives attended and worked at.
According to Alarabiya, the school teacher, one of the wives, told newspaper Okaz that the way she interacted with her husband’s other wives at work was “no different” to how she treated other students and superiors.
To sum up, the claim that a Saudi man married a student, a teacher and the principal, all from the same school and, has a fourth wife who was an educational supervisor is true, but not a recent incident. This happened around 12 years ago, in 2012. The image that is being circulated along with this claim is inauthentic and AI-generated.
claim_text: Image shows Saudi with four wives: a student, a teacher, the principal and an educational supervisor all from the same school.
original_post_text: Some Crazy images from the Past.🧵 ||| Islam For You!! pic.twitter.com/5D6ivJNpDq

title: Did J P Nadda insult the Constitution by placing it near his feet? Clipped video viral with misleading claims
body_text:
A video shared on X by the official handle of the Indian Youth Congress purportedly shows BJP national president and Union health minister J P Nadda placing a copy of the Constitution of India near his feet while addressing the Rajya Sabha. The Congress youth wing condemned the move and remarked, “Why does BJP hate Babasaheb Ambedkar’s Constitution so much(?)” and demanded an apology. The tweet garnered 140000 views. (Archive)
🚨 #BREAKING :
👉 आज राज्य सभा में संविधान को अपने पैरों के पास रखते नजर आए भाजपा के राष्ट्रीय अध्यक्ष JP Nadda!!आखिर BJP को बाबासाहेब अंबेडकर के संविधान से इतनी नफरत क्यों है ⁉️
देश को आपकी माफी का इंतजार है JP Nadda जी!! pic.twitter.com/IZGOZLRZP7
— Indian Youth Congress (@IYC) February 11, 2025
Congress leader Srinivas BV also tweeted the same video and sarcastically asked, “Honorable Nadda Ji… do you keep the Constitution under your feet?”. (Archive)
माननीय नड्डा जी…संविधान को पैरों में रखते है?pic.twitter.com/DomAr72lLA
— Srinivas BV (@srinivasiyc) February 11, 2025
‘Political Commentator’ Saibpal Pandit tweeted the same video with the same claim. (Archive)
🚨 SHAME ALERT
BJP National President JP Nadda was seen placing the Constitution near his feet in the Rajya Sabha today.
Why does the BJ Party continue to disrespect Babasaheb Ambedkar’s Constitution ? pic.twitter.com/oT1xjkdO1S
— Saibpal Pandit (@PanditSaibpal) February 11, 2025
Several handles, including some linked to the Congress IT Cell, like Kerala Congress Sevadal and Telangana Youth Congress, amplified the viral video alongside the viral claim. (Archives- 1, 2, 3, 4)
Fact Check
Alt News found that the clip was taken from the live stream of the Rajya Sabha proceedings on February 11. At the 20:33 mark of the ANI livestream, Nadda lifts a large copy of the Constitution to display it to the House. While doing so, he fumbles with it briefly and appears to place it under the table, at which point the camera shifts to Rajya Sabha chairperson and vice president Jagdeep Dhankhar.
After the clip went viral on social media, several users defended Nadda by sharing a full video of the sequence of events. In this footage, Nadda is seen adjusting the large-sized Constitution under the table, placing it on the table, and then lifting it again to display it to the House. The video was also shared by Rajya Sabha MP Sudhanshu Trivedi.
संविधान के मूल प्रारूप में से प्रेरणा के प्रतीक चित्रों को हटाकर संविधान निर्माताओं के मूल भाव को नष्ट करने का प्रयास करने वाले वे लोग आज जो मुखौटा लगाकर संविधान की बात कर रहे हैं आज संसद में भारतीय जनता पार्टी और हमारे राष्ट्रीय अध्यक्ष श्री जगत प्रकाश नड्डा जी के द्वारा उठाए गए… pic.twitter.com/DouFEDui4K
— Dr. Sudhanshu Trivedi (@SudhanshuTrived) February 11, 2025
We have added the sequence of events below. The last two frames in the sequence have been left out of the video that has been made to go viral.
While it is not possible to ascertain whether the copy of the Constitution had slipped from Nadda’s hand or he had deliberately placed it below the table, it is indeed clear that he immediately picked it up or stopped before placing it on the floor and eventually kept it on his desk. Several Congress leaders and Congress-linked pages shared a clipped video with misleading claims.
claim_text: J P Nadda insulted the Constitution by placing it near his feet

title: ‘Cat’ in the act: After row over meat found in Hindu temple, probe reveals a feline fault
body_text:
After a video showed pieces of meat scattered inside a temple premises in Hyderabad, claims have emerged that it was a deliberate act to disrupt the sanctity of a Hindu place of worship. The incident took place at the Jirra Hanuman Temple in the Tappachabutra locality of old city Hyderabad on February 12.
Some social media users alleged that unidentified people jumped inside the temple from the backside and threw chunks of meat near the Shiva Linga, which represents the Hindu deity Shiva. Others highlighted that the incident happened in a constituency represented by a Muslim politician — AIMIM chief Asaduddin Owaisi.
The day the video emerged, Bharatiya Janata Party MLA from Telangana, Raja Singh, shared the clip and called it a “deliberate act of provocation” by “anti-social elements” under Congress rule. (Archive)
Note that the MLA, who also goes by T Raja Singh, has a history of delivering hateful speeches targeting the Muslim community. He was suspended from the BJP for nearly a year after a major row over his remarks on Prophet Mohammad. However, he was on the list of the party’s official campaigners during the general election last year where he delivered hate speeches, referring to Muslims as “Pakistani Mullo” and threatening to throw them out of the country.
A deliberate act of provocation is unacceptable.
Under Congress rule, once again, anti-social elements have attempted to disrupt peace in the Tappachabutra area in the old city of #Hyderabad by targeting the Hanuman Mandir. pic.twitter.com/de3Xf3fMTS
— Raja Singh (@TigerRajaSingh) February 12, 2025
Media outlet NewsMeter (@NewsMeter_In) posted two clips from the temple on February 12. One showed the area where the Shiva Linga was present and the other showed individuals including the police gathered inside the premises. The caption said that pieces of meat were allegedly thrown inside the temple by some people. (Archive)
Tension prevailed for a while at #Tappachabutra on Wednesday after some persons allegedly threw pieces of #meat in a #temple.
After noticing pieces of flesh within the temple premises, the priest of #Hanuman temple alerted the committee members who informed to… pic.twitter.com/G8JSlZx81g
— NewsMeter (@NewsMeter_In) February 12, 2025
Another X user @YakkatiSowmith, reportedly a Times Now journalist as per his bio, shared the same clip showing the Shiva Linga and the meat and said that “unidentified people allegedly jumped into the temple from behind and threw meat chunk near Shiva Linga”. (Archive)
A chunk of Meat found in a #Temple near Lord Shiva Linga in #Hyderabad’s old city. Some unidentified people allegedly jumped into the temple from behind and threw meat chunk near Shiva Linga in Tappachabutra. Police reached the spot and looking into the matter. pic.twitter.com/FYTNB7J5C1
— Sowmith Yakkati (@YakkatiSowmith) February 12, 2025
Below are more instances of similar claims.
Fact Check
To look into the claims, we first did a keyword search that led us to several news reports on the incident. A report by India Today, published on February 12 around 2 pm, said that the police have begun their investigation and are closely examining CCTV footage. According to Deputy Commissioner of Police South-West, Chandra Mohan, who was cited in the report, since all the doors of the temple were locked, it was likely that the bits of meat were brought into the premises by an animal.
However, BJP MLA Raja Singh dismissed this theory even before the investigation was complete. He claimed that such incidents have become frequent in the city and accused the police of routinely attributing them to stray animals, like dogs or cats.
In an X post around 8:30 pm on February 12, the Tappachabutra area police shared a press note and a video statement by the station house officer along with the CCTV footage revealing the culprit. Footage retrieved from the north-facing camera of the temple clearly showed a cat carrying a piece of meat in its mouth and entering the temple premises, the police said. In the press note, they urged citizens to refrain from amplifying rumours and misinformation related to this incident.
— SHO TAPPACHABUTRA (@shotappachbutra) February 12, 2025
A report by The Indian Express published the same day around 11:04 pm, said that CCTV footage from 17 cameras that covered the area was used to ascertain that no one entered the temple between the night of February 11 and the morning of February 12. Thus the cat was conclusively guilty.
Therefore, rumours that the Hindu temple was desecrated deliberately and that unidentified individuals threw meat inside the premises are baseless and false as confirmed by police investigation.
claim_text: Unindentified individuals entered the Jirra Hanuman Temple in Tappachabutra, Hyderabad, and threw meat near the Shiva Lingam
original_post_text: A deliberate act of provocation is unacceptable. ||| #Hyderabad — ||| A chunk of Meat found in a #Temple near Lord Shiva Linga in #Hyderabad ’s old city. Some unidentified people allegedly jumped into the temple from behind and threw meat chunk near Shiva Linga in Tappachabutra. Police reached the spot and looking into the matter. pic.twitter.com/FYTNB7J5C1 ||| pic.twitter.com/f2XVqugD1v

title: Video of Congress leaders dancing after launch of Delhi polls theme song peddled as post-election celebrations
body_text:
Amid the backdrop of the 2025 Delhi assembly election results, a video featuring Congress leaders dancing is being widely shared on social media, claiming that despite failing to secure any seats, Congress leaders were celebrating.
Congress, which was in power in Delhi for 15 years from 1998 to 2013, failed to win a single seat a third time in a row in Delhi. Voting was held for all constituencies on February 5 and results were announced on February 8.
ABPLive uploaded the purported video clip with a caption that, “Delhi Election Result: Congress is celebrating their 4.5% votes share in Delhi”.
Right-wing influencer Raushan Sinha shared the video on X (@MrSinha_) with the claim that there was a celebration happening at the Congress headquarters for maintaining their previous election result of securing nil seats at Delhi assembly. (Archive)
Celebration at Congress HQ as they maintain their previous result: Zero.
🤣🤣🤣
pic.twitter.com/po72mXhi5z— Mr Sinha (@MrSinha_) February 8, 2025
The post garnered around 2,45,700 views.
Another Right-wing user, OpIndia CEO Rahul Roushan (@rahulroushan), too, made a similar claim. (Archive)
Congress leaders celebrating the fact that the party didn’t lose any seat from last time’s tally pic.twitter.com/JFWqY7HC9p
— Rahul Roushan (@rahulroushan) February 8, 2025
Several other social media accounts have made similar claims. A few of them are in the gallery below:
Fact Check
The first thing we noticed was that Rahul Roushan and Mr. Sinha had reshared posts from January by users Mohit Raj Dubey and Radhika Khera respectively. This clearly shows that the video predates the recently held Delhi assembly elections.
Then we performed a reverse image search with some key frames from the video. This led us to several posts on social media platforms from January 2025, that is much before the elections. A tweet by Ajay Jha dated January 23 said, “Beyond victory and defeat… Congress launched its ‘theme song’ for the Delhi assembly elections with drums and trumpets. Senior leaders of the party also danced to the theme song, boosting the enthusiasm of the workers.”
जीत हार से परे..कांग्रेस पार्टी ने ढोल नगाड़े के साथ दिल्ली विधानसभा चुनाव के लिए अपने “थीम सॉन्ग” को लॉन्च किया..पार्टी के वरिष्ठ नेताओं ने भी थीम सॉन्ग पर थिडक कर कार्यकर्ताओं का उत्साह बढ़ाया.. pic.twitter.com/Ii0Eav5CgC
— Ajay Jha (@Ajay_reporter) January 23, 2025
Taking cue from this, we performed a keyword search that led us to a video report by NDTV India on January 23. The caption read, “Congress launched its theme song for the Delhi Elections, with party leaders dancing to the tune”. The video report features similar frames along with the entire theme song.
During our investigation we also found that the YouTube channel of Indian National Congress had live streamed the launch of their official theme song for Delhi assembly elections.
The Times of India also reported on January 23 that ahead of Delhi assembly polls, Congress had released a new campaign song titled, “Har jarurat hogi puri, Dilli meh Congress hai jaruri” (Every need will be fulfilled, Delhi needs Congress).
To sum up, the video clip of Congress leaders dancing is from the launch of the party’s official campaign song on January 23, 2025. It has no relation with the Delhi assembly election results.
claim_text: Congress leaders celebrated Delhi assembly election results by dancing
original_post_text: Celebration at Congress HQ as they maintain their previous result: Zero. ||| Congress leaders celebrating the fact that the party didn’t lose any seat from last time’s tally pic.twitter.com/JFWqY7HC9p ||| जीत हार से परे..कांग्रेस पार्टी ने ढोल नगाड़े के साथ दिल्ली विधानसभा चुनाव के लिए अपने “थीम सॉन्ग” को लॉन्च किया..पार्टी के वरिष्ठ नेताओं ने भी थीम सॉन्ग पर थिडक कर कार्यकर्ताओं का उत्साह बढ़ाया.. pic.twitter.com/Ii0Eav5CgC

title: Sudarshan News invents communal angle in Faridabad murder; falsely reports that accused were Muslims
body_text:
Trigger Warning: Graphic Descriptions of Violence
On January 9, 2025, Right-wing propaganda outlet Sudarshan News ran a video showing a deceased young man as a ‘breaking news’ post on its X handle and YouTube channel, and claimed that Jihadis had killed a Hindu youth in ‘Taliban style’. The outlet claimed the victim’s fingers and toes were severed, eyes and nails were pulled out, and his head was separated from his body. Along with this, Sudarshan News uploaded another video on its YouTube channel with a similar claim in which mentioned that that Deepak was killed because he had witnessed the rape of a Hindu girl by the son of the accused, Fakruddin, and his 12 friends. (Archived link 1, link 2, link 3)
जिहादियों ने की हिंदू युवक की अमानवीय हत्या!
26 साल के हिंदू युवक को पहले किडनैप किया गया, फिर हाथ-पैर की उँगली काटी और सर को धड़ से अलग कर दिया!
तालिबानी अंदाज़ में की गई हरियाणा के फ़रीदाबाद में हिंदू युवक की भयानक हत्या!#Delhi #Jihadi #Hindu pic.twitter.com/QWSpMNc5iA
— Sudarshan News (@SudarshanNewsTV) January 9, 2025
Readers should note that Alt News has fact-checked the false and communal claims circulated by Sudarshan News several times in the past.
Facebook user Ravi Shukla shared the video report by Sudarshan News and made a similar claim. (Archived link)
Deepak Sharma, a verified X handle often found spreading false communal information, shared a photo and claimed that Deepak was brutally murdered by Anwar, Abdul and Rashid in Faridabad, Haryana, because Deepak had become a witness in a rape case against them. (Archived link)
फिर एक हिन्दू
हैवानियत भरी जिहाद का शिकार हुआ….ये दीपक है इसे जिहादी अनवर अब्दुल और रशीद ने पहले अपहरण किया, फिर 15 दिन इसपर पेशाब करते रहे, फिर धीरे धीरे इसकी उंगलियां काटी नाखून खींचे… लिंग काटा दाँत तोड़े जीभ काटी और फिर गर्दन साफ कर दी
आप इस हैवान भीड़ को अगर इंसान… pic.twitter.com/NDUAxVQUG7
— Deepak Sharma (@SonOfBharat7) January 9, 2025
Several other verified X handles including @NadeemHere_ and Avkush Singh Malik also shared photos and videos with similar claims. (Archived link 1, link 2)
Fact Check
Alt News observed that the Faridabad Police, in its reply to a now-deleted tweet by Avkush Singh Malik, wrote that Deepak was not involved in any gangrape case. They clarified there were no eyewitnesses in the incident and requested social media users to not spread any misleading news by linking it to a particular community, as doing so may have a negative impact on the society.
दीपक किसी भी गैंगरेप मामले में आईविटनेस नहीं था कृपया करके समुदाय से जोड़कर कोई भी भ्रामक खबर न फैलाए जिससे समाज पर बुरा असर पड़े,दीपक हत्या के मामले में पुलिस की टीम जांच मै जुटी हुई है
— People’s Police – Faridabad Police (@FBDPolice) January 9, 2025
Following this, we performed a keyword search, which led us to a news report by Dainik Bhaskar from January 13, 2025. According to this report, this case is from Faridabad’s Adarsh Nagar where the body of Deepak, who had gone missing on December 16, 2024, was recovered on January 8, 2025. Faridabad Police arrested Deepak’s friend Karan on charges of murdering him.
Assistant commissioner (Crime) Aman Yadav told Dainik Bhaskar that Deepak’s brother Dilip had filed a missing report on December 17. He stated that Deepak had left home on December 16 and did not return. The complainant accused 14 Muslims in the area of murder.
The report also states that Deepak and the accused, Karan, were friends. Karan reportedly had two wives, and he suspected Deepak of having a relationship with his first wife, due to which the accused and the deceased had a quarrel in the past. According to police, Karan got Deepak drunk, hit him on the head, strangled him with a scarf and threw his body in the bushes.
Faridabad Police tweeted a video on January 14, 2025 and revealed that Deepak’s friend Karan has been arrested in his murder case. Deepak was killed due to mutual enmity and an alleged illicit relationship with Karan’s first wife.
दीपक हत्या मामले मे फरीदाबाद पुलिस को मिली बड़ी #कामयाबी
आदर्श नगर एरिया में हुई दीपक हत्या के मामले का खुलासा, #अपराध शाखा DLF की टीम ने मुख्या आरोपी करण को किया काबू
दीपक का #दोस्त करण ही निकला #हत्यारा!#MurderAccusedArrested #PoliceCrackdownOnCrime #MurderInvestigation… pic.twitter.com/pRAsg3XdvK— People’s Police – Faridabad Police (@FBDPolice) January 14, 2025
Along with this, we also came across an updated report by Dainik Bhaskar related to this case from January 19, 2025. According to this report, a second accused named Pramod alias Golu had been arrested for Deepak’s murder. Pramod admitted that he and Karan had killed Deepak by hitting him on the head with a stone.
To sum it up, Deepak was killed by his friends Karan and Pramod due to personal enmity and both accused are Hindus, but Sudarshan News shared this incident with a false communal spin.
claim_text: Faridabad youth Deepak killed by Muslims in Taliban style
